Genesis.q: Who was the first person created by God?
Genesis.e: Genesis 2:7 — God formed man from the dust of the ground.

Genesis.q: Out of what was Eve created?
Genesis.e: Genesis 2:21-22 — God took one of Adam's ribs and made it into a woman.

Genesis.q: What was the first thing God created?
Genesis.e: Genesis 1:3 — God said, 'Let there be light.'

Genesis.q: How many days did God take to create the world?
Genesis.e: Genesis 1:31-2:2 — God finished His work on the sixth day and rested on the seventh.

Genesis.q: Who was the oldest man mentioned in Genesis?
Genesis.e: Genesis 5:27 — Methuselah lived 969 years.

Genesis.q: Which son of Adam killed his brother?
Genesis.e: Genesis 4:8 — Cain killed his brother Abel.

Genesis.q: What was the sign of God's covenant with Noah?
Genesis.e: Genesis 9:13 — God set His rainbow in the clouds as a sign of the covenant.

Genesis.q: How many people were on the ark?
Genesis.e: Genesis 7:13 — Noah, his wife, his three sons, and their wives entered the ark.

Genesis.q: What was the name of the tower built to reach heaven?
Genesis.e: Genesis 11:4-9 — The people tried to build a tower to the heavens, so God confused their language.

Genesis.q: Who was the father of the twelve tribes of Israel?
Genesis.e: Genesis 35:23-26 — Jacob, also named Israel, had twelve sons who became the tribes.

Genesis.q: Who was Isaac's wife?
Genesis.e: Genesis 24:67 — Isaac married Rebekah and loved her.

Genesis.q: What did Esau sell his birthright for?
Genesis.e: Genesis 25:34 — Esau sold his birthright for bread and lentil stew.

Genesis.q: What new name did God give Jacob?
Genesis.e: Genesis 32:28 — Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel.

Genesis.q: Which of Jacob's sons was sold into slavery?
Genesis.e: Genesis 37:28 — Joseph's brothers sold him to Midianite traders.

Genesis.q: Who was Joseph's father?
Genesis.e: Genesis 37:3 — Now Israel (Jacob) loved Joseph more than any of his other sons.

Genesis.q: Who tempted Eve in the Garden of Eden?
Genesis.e: Genesis 3:1 — The serpent was more crafty than any of the wild animals.

Genesis.q: What was the first bird Noah sent out from the ark?
Genesis.e: Genesis 8:7 — He sent out a raven, and it kept flying back and forth.

Genesis.q: Who was Sarah's maidservant who had a son with Abraham?
Genesis.e: Genesis 16:1-3 — Sarai gave her Egyptian maidservant Hagar to Abram.

Genesis.q: What was the name of Hagar's son?
Genesis.e: Genesis 16:15 — Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram named him Ishmael.

Genesis.q: What city was Lot living in when it was destroyed by fire?
Genesis.e: Genesis 19:24-25 — The Lord rained down burning sulfur on Sodom and Gomorrah.

Genesis.q: What happened to Lot's wife when she looked back?
o: She turned into a pillar of salt
Genesis.e: Genesis 19:26 — Lot's wife looked back and became a pillar of salt.

Genesis.q: How old was Abraham when Isaac was born?
Genesis.e: Genesis 21:5 — Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born.

Genesis.q: Whom did God tell Abraham to sacrifice on a mountain?
Genesis.e: Genesis 22:2 — God said, 'Take your son, your only son Isaac... and sacrifice him.'

Genesis.q: What did Jacob see in his dream at Bethel?
Genesis.e: Genesis 28:12 — He had a dream in which he saw a stairway (ladder) resting on the earth.

Genesis.q: Which son of Jacob was the oldest?
Genesis.e: Genesis 29:32 — Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben.

Genesis.q: Who was Jacob's favorite wife?
Genesis.e: Genesis 29:30 — Jacob loved Rachel more than Leah.

Genesis.q: How many years did Jacob work for Laban to marry Rachel?
Genesis.e: Genesis 29:18-30 — Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and then another seven.

Genesis.q: What was the name of the place where Jacob wrestled with God?
Genesis.e: Genesis 32:30 — Jacob called the place Peniel, saying, 'I saw God face to face.'

Genesis.q: Who bought Joseph as a slave in Egypt?
Genesis.e: Genesis 39:1 — Potiphar, an Egyptian who was one of Pharaoh’s officials, bought him.

Genesis.q: Whose dream did Joseph interpret while in prison?
Genesis.e: Genesis 40:5-19 — Joseph interpreted the dreams of the cupbearer and the baker.

Genesis.q: How many years of famine did Joseph predict?
Genesis.e: Genesis 41:29-30 — Seven years of great abundance will come, followed by seven years of famine.

Genesis.q: Which brother stayed behind in prison while the others returned to Canaan?
Genesis.e: Genesis 42:24 — Joseph took Simeon from them and had him bound before their eyes.

Genesis.q: Which brother offered to take Benjamin's place as Joseph's slave?
Genesis.e: Genesis 44:33 — Judah offered to stay as Joseph's slave instead of the boy Benjamin.

Genesis.q: In what land did the Israelites settle in Egypt?
Genesis.e: Genesis 47:27 — Now the Israelites settled in Egypt in the region of Goshen.

Genesis.q: Who was the first woman?
Genesis.e: Genesis 3:20 — Adam named his wife Eve, because she would be the mother of all the living.

Genesis.q: What was the name of the garden where Adam and Eve lived?
Genesis.e: Genesis 2:8 — Now the Lord God had planted a garden in the east, in Eden.

Genesis.q: On what day did God create the sun, moon, and stars?
Genesis.e: Genesis 1:14-19 — God made two great lights and the stars on the fourth day.

Genesis.q: Who walked with God and then was no more because God took him?
Genesis.e: Genesis 5:24 — Enoch walked faithfully with God; then he was no more, because God took him away.

Genesis.q: What kind of wood was used to build the ark?
Genesis.e: Genesis 6:14 — So make yourself an ark of cypress (gopher) wood.

Genesis.q: Where did the ark come to rest?
Genesis.e: Genesis 8:4 — The ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.

Genesis.q: Who was Abraham's father?
Genesis.e: Genesis 11:27 — Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran.

Genesis.q: Who was Lot's uncle?
Genesis.e: Genesis 12:5 — Abram took his wife Sarai, his nephew Lot...

Genesis.q: How many righteous people were needed to save Sodom?
Genesis.e: Genesis 18:32 — God said, 'For the sake of ten, I will not destroy it.'

Genesis.q: What was the name of Isaac's firstborn son?
Genesis.e: Genesis 25:25 — The first to come out was red... so they named him Esau.

Genesis.q: Who was Laban's sister and Isaac's wife?
Genesis.e: Genesis 24:29 — Rebekah had a brother named Laban.

Genesis.q: Which brother suggested selling Joseph rather than killing him?
Genesis.e: Genesis 37:26-27 — Judah said, 'What will we gain if we kill our brother? Let’s sell him.'

Genesis.q: What did Joseph's brothers dip his coat in to deceive their father?
Genesis.e: Genesis 37:31 — Then they got Joseph’s robe, slaughtered a goat and dipped the robe in the blood.

Genesis.q: What was the name of Joseph's wife in Egypt?
Genesis.e: Genesis 41:45 — Pharaoh gave him Asenath daughter of Poti-Phera... to be his wife.

Genesis.q: Who were Joseph's two sons?
Genesis.e: Genesis 41:50-52 — Before the years of famine came, two sons were born to Joseph... Manasseh and Ephraim.

Genesis.q: How old was Jacob when he died?
Genesis.e: Genesis 47:28 — Jacob lived in Egypt seventeen years, and the years of his life were 147.

Genesis.q: Which woman was turned into a pillar of salt?
Genesis.e: Genesis 19:26 — But Lot’s wife looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.

Genesis.q: Which woman was the mother of Ishmael?
Genesis.e: Genesis 16:15 — So Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram gave the name Ishmael.

Genesis.q: Who was the wife of Lot?
Genesis.e: Genesis 19:26 refers to her only as 'Lot's wife'.

Genesis.q: Which woman was the wife of Nahor and daughter of Haran?
Genesis.e: Genesis 11:29 — The name of Nahor’s wife was Milcah; she was the daughter of Haran.

Genesis.q: Which woman was the mother of Dan and Naphtali?
Genesis.e: Genesis 30:4-8 — Bilhah... gave birth to a son... and she gave birth to a second son.

Genesis.q: Who was the mother of Gad and Asher?
Genesis.e: Genesis 30:9-13 — Zilpah... gave birth to a son... and she gave birth to a second son.

Genesis.q: Which woman was the wife of Ham and was on the ark?
Genesis.e: Genesis 7:13 — Noah and his sons... together with his wife and the wives of his three sons.

Genesis.q: Who was the wife of Shem and was on the ark?
Genesis.e: Genesis 7:13 — The wives of his three sons.

Genesis.q: Which woman was the wife of Japheth and was on the ark?
Genesis.e: Genesis 7:13 — The wives of his three sons.

Genesis.q: Which animal is mentioned as being 'cursed' above all livestock in Genesis 3?
Genesis.e: Genesis 3:14 — Cursed are you above all livestock and all wild animals! You will crawl on your belly.

Genesis.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Naphtali?
Genesis.e: Genesis 49:21 — Naphtali is a doe set free.

Genesis.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Zebulun?
Genesis.e: Genesis 49:13 — Zebulun will live by the seashore.

Genesis.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Reuben?
Genesis.e: Genesis 49:3-4 — Reuben... unstable as water.

Genesis.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Simeon and Levi?
Genesis.e: Genesis 49:5 — Simeon and Levi are brothers—their swords are weapons of violence.

Genesis.q: What river flows out of the Garden of Eden and splits into four?
o: The river in Eden (Pishon, Gihon, Tigris, Euphrates)
Genesis.e: Genesis 2:10 — A river watering the garden flowed from Eden; from there it was separated into four headwaters.

Genesis.q: Where did God confuse the languages of the people?
Genesis.e: Genesis 11:9 — That is why it was called Babel.

Genesis.q: Where did Jacob have his dream of a ladder to heaven?
Genesis.e: Genesis 28:19 — He called that place Bethel.

Genesis.q: Which city did the Lord destroy along with Gomorrah?
Genesis.e: Genesis 19:24 — The Lord rained down burning sulfur on Sodom and Gomorrah.

Genesis.q: Where was the 'Garden of Eden' said to be located (general region)?
Genesis.e: Genesis 2:8 — Now the Lord God had planted a garden in the east, in Eden.

Genesis.q: Where did the Lord appear to Jacob and he wrestled with him?
Genesis.e: Genesis 32:30 — Jacob called the place Peniel, saying, 'I saw God face to face.'

Exodus.q: Where did God speak to Moses from the burning bush?
Exodus.e: Exodus 3:1 — Moses came to Horeb, the mountain of God.

Exodus.q: What was the first plague God sent upon Egypt?
Exodus.e: Exodus 7:20 — All the water in the Nile was changed into blood.

Exodus.q: How many plagues did God send upon Egypt in total?
Exodus.e: Exodus 7-12 — There were ten plagues starting with blood and ending with the death of the firstborn.

Exodus.q: What was the final plague?
Exodus.e: Exodus 11:5 — Every firstborn son in Egypt will die.

Exodus.q: Through which body of water did the Israelites cross on dry land?
Exodus.e: Exodus 14:21-22 — The Lord drove the sea back... and the Israelites went through the sea on dry ground.

Exodus.q: What food did God provide from heaven for the Israelites in the wilderness?
Exodus.e: Exodus 16:14-15 — When the dew was gone, thin flakes like frost appeared... they called it manna.

Exodus.q: On what mountain did Moses receive the Ten Commandments?
Exodus.e: Exodus 19:20 — The Lord descended to the top of Mount Sinai and called Moses.

Exodus.q: What was the first commandment?
o: You shall have no other gods before me
Exodus.e: Exodus 20:3 — You shall have no other gods before me.

Exodus.q: Who was Moses' brother?
Exodus.e: Exodus 4:14 — Is there not your brother, Aaron the Levite?

Exodus.q: What did Aaron make for the people to worship while Moses was on the mountain?
Exodus.e: Exodus 32:4 — He took what they handed him and made it into an idol cast in the shape of a calf.

Exodus.q: Who was Moses' sister?
Exodus.e: Exodus 15:20 — Then Miriam the prophet, Aaron’s sister, took a timbrel in her hand.

Exodus.q: What was the name of the portable tent where God's presence dwelt?
Exodus.e: Exodus 25-27 — God gave instructions for building the Tabernacle.

Exodus.q: How long did the Israelites wander in the wilderness?
Exodus.e: Exodus 16:35 — The Israelites ate manna forty years.

Exodus.q: What was the name of Moses' wife?
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:21 — Moses agreed to stay with the man, who gave his daughter Zipporah to Moses.

Exodus.q: Who was the father-in-law of Moses?
Exodus.e: Exodus 3:1 — Moses was tending the flock of Jethro his father-in-law.

Exodus.q: What did Moses' staff turn into when he threw it on the ground?
Exodus.e: Exodus 4:3 — Moses threw it on the ground and it became a snake.

Exodus.q: What did the Israelites eat on the night of the first Passover?
Exodus.e: Exodus 12:8 — That same night they are to eat the meat roasted... along with bitter herbs and bread made without yeast.

Exodus.q: Why did Moses' mother hide him in a basket in the Nile?
o: To save him from Pharaoh's order to kill Hebrew baby boys
To hide him from his sister
To help him find Pharaoh's daughter
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:2-3 — When she could hide him no longer, she got a papyrus basket...

Exodus.q: Who found baby Moses in the river?
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:5 — Pharaoh’s daughter went down to the Nile to bathe... she saw the basket among the reeds.

Exodus.q: What was the name of Moses' son?
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:22 — Zipporah gave birth to a son, and Moses named him Gershom.

Exodus.q: How many years were the Israelites in Egypt?
Exodus.e: Exodus 12:40 — Now the length of time the Israelite people lived in Egypt was 430 years.

Exodus.q: Which plague involved insects that covered the land?
Exodus.e: Exodus 8:16-17 — All the dust throughout the land of Egypt became gnats.

Exodus.q: What did the Israelites have to put on their doorposts to be spared from the final plague?
Exodus.e: Exodus 12:7 — They are to take some of the blood and put it on the sides and tops of the doorframes.

Exodus.q: Which tribe did Moses and Aaron belong to?
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:1 — Now a man of the tribe of Levi married a Levite woman.

Exodus.q: How did God lead the Israelites by day?
Exodus.e: Exodus 13:21 — By day the Lord went ahead of them in a pillar of cloud.

Exodus.q: How did God lead the Israelites by night?
Exodus.e: Exodus 13:21 — By night [the Lord went ahead of them] in a pillar of fire to give them light.

Exodus.q: Who was Moses' successor who led the people into the Promised Land?
Exodus.e: Exodus 17:9, Joshua 1 — Joshua was Moses' aide and led the battle against the Amalekites.

Exodus.q: What did Moses do to make the bitter water at Marah sweet?
o: He threw a piece of wood into it
He struck it with his staff
Exodus.e: Exodus 15:25 — Then the Lord showed him a piece of wood. He threw it into the water, and the water became fit to drink.

Exodus.q: What did Moses have to do to ensure the Israelites won the battle against the Amalekites?
Exodus.e: Exodus 17:11 — As long as Moses held up his hands, the Israelites were winning.

Exodus.q: Who helped Moses by holding up his arms during the battle?
Exodus.e: Exodus 17:12 — Aaron and Hur held his hands up—one on one side, one on the other.

Exodus.q: What was inside the Ark of the Covenant?
Exodus.e: Exodus 25:16, Hebrews 9:4 — The Ark contained the tablets, manna, and Aaron's rod.

Exodus.q: What was the name of the place where Moses struck a rock to get water?
Exodus.e: Exodus 17:7 — He called the place Massah and Meribah because the Israelites quarreled.

Exodus.q: Who was the primary craftsman for the Tabernacle?
Exodus.e: Exodus 31:1-2 — I have chosen Bezalel son of Uri... and I have filled him with the Spirit of God.

Exodus.q: What happened to Moses' face after he spoke with God on the mountain?
Exodus.e: Exodus 34:29 — He was not aware that his face was radiant because he had spoken with the Lord.

Exodus.q: How many of the Ten Commandments were about our relationship with God?
Exodus.e: Exodus 20:1-11 — The first four commandments deal with God; the last six deal with others.

Exodus.q: What was the punishment for the Israelites' worship of the golden calf?
o: They were sent back to Egypt
Exodus.e: Exodus 32:28 — The Levites did as Moses commanded, and that day about three thousand of the people died.

Exodus.q: Who did God appoint to be Moses' spokesman?
Exodus.e: Exodus 4:14-16 — He will speak to the people for you.

Exodus.q: What was the tenth commandment?
Exodus.e: Exodus 20:17 — You shall not covet your neighbor’s house.

Exodus.q: Which plague came immediately after the gnats?
Exodus.e: Exodus 8:20-24 — The fourth plague was the plague of flies.

Exodus.q: What happened to Pharaoh's army when they chased Israel into the sea?
Exodus.e: Exodus 14:28 — The water flowed back and covered the chariots and horsemen—the entire army of Pharaoh.

Exodus.q: How often were the Israelites to gather manna?
Exodus.e: Exodus 16:4 — The people are to go out each day and gather enough for that day.

Exodus.q: What happened to manna if it was kept until the next morning (except on Friday)?
Exodus.e: Exodus 16:20 — However, some of them paid no attention... it was full of maggots and began to smell.

Exodus.q: What was the eighth plague?
Exodus.e: Exodus 10:4 — I will bring locusts into your country tomorrow.

Exodus.q: What was the seventh plague?
Exodus.e: Exodus 9:18 — I will send the worst hailstorm that has ever fallen on Egypt.

Exodus.q: How many years old was Moses when he first stood before Pharaoh?
Exodus.e: Exodus 7:7 — Moses was eighty years old and Aaron eighty-three when they spoke to Pharaoh.

Exodus.q: What did the Israelites use for the gold of the golden calf?
o: Gold they found in the desert
Exodus.e: Exodus 32:2-3 — Aaron answered them, 'Take off the gold earrings...' So all the people took off their earrings.

Exodus.q: Which commandment requires honoring your parents?
Exodus.e: Exodus 20:12 — Honor your father and your mother, so that you may live long.

Exodus.q: Where did Moses flee to after killing an Egyptian?
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:15 — Moses fled from Pharaoh and went to live in Midian.

Exodus.q: What was the name of the assistant to Bezalel in building the Tabernacle?
Exodus.e: Exodus 31:6 — Moreover, I have appointed Oholiab son of Ahisamak... to help him.

Exodus.q: How long did the plague of darkness last?
Exodus.e: Exodus 10:22-23 — Total darkness covered all Egypt for three days.

Exodus.q: Who was the wife of Moses?
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:21 — Moses agreed to stay with the man, who gave his daughter Zipporah to Moses.

Exodus.q: Where did the Israelites camp before they crossed the Red Sea?
Exodus.e: Exodus 14:2 — Tell the Israelites to turn back and encamp near Pi Hahiroth.

Exodus.q: What was the punishment for kidnapping a person to sell them as a slave?
o: Working as a slave for the victim
Exodus.e: Exodus 21:16 — Anyone who kidnaps someone is to be put to death.

Exodus.q: What sign was given to a slave who chose to stay with his master for life?
o: An ear pierced with an awl
Exodus.e: Exodus 21:6 — Then his master shall take him to the doorpost and pierce his ear with an awl.

Exodus.q: What was the penalty for striking one's father or mother?
Exodus.e: Exodus 21:15 — Anyone who attacks their father or mother is to be put to death.

Exodus.q: Which festival was also known as the Festival of Weeks?
Exodus.e: Exodus 34:22 — Celebrate the Festival of Weeks with the firstfruits of the wheat harvest.

Exodus.q: How many plagues did God send on Egypt?
Exodus.e: Exodus 7-12

Exodus.q: What was the first plague?
Exodus.e: Exodus 7:20

Exodus.q: What was the last plague?
Exodus.e: Exodus 11:5

Exodus.q: What body of water did the Israelites cross?
Exodus.e: Exodus 14

Exodus.q: Who was Moses' spokesman?
Exodus.e: Exodus 4:14

Exodus.q: What food did God provide in the morning?
Exodus.e: Exodus 16:15

Exodus.q: What food did God provide in the evening?
Exodus.e: Exodus 16:13

Exodus.q: On what mountain were the Ten Commandments given?
Exodus.e: Exodus 19:20

Exodus.q: What did Aaron make while Moses was on the mountain?
Exodus.e: Exodus 32:4

Exodus.q: How long did the Israelites wander in the wilderness?
Exodus.e: Exodus 16:35

Exodus.q: What was the name of the tent where God dwelt?
Exodus.e: Exodus 25:8

Exodus.q: Who found baby Moses in the river?
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:5

Exodus.q: Why did Moses flee Egypt?
o: He wanted to find his family
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:12-15

Exodus.q: Where did God speak to Moses from a bush?
Exodus.e: Exodus 3:1

Exodus.q: What was Moses' staff turned into?
Exodus.e: Exodus 4:3

Exodus.q: What sign did Moses show Pharaoh involving his hand?
Exodus.e: Exodus 4:6

Exodus.q: Who was Moses' wife?
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:21

Exodus.q: Who was Moses' father-in-law?
Exodus.e: Exodus 3:1

Exodus.q: What did the Israelites have to put on their doorposts for Passover?
Exodus.e: Exodus 12:7

Exodus.q: How many years were the Israelites in Egypt?
Exodus.e: Exodus 12:40

Exodus.q: How did God lead them by day?
Exodus.e: Exodus 13:21

Exodus.q: How did God lead them by night?
Exodus.e: Exodus 13:21

Exodus.q: Who sang a song after crossing the Red Sea?
Exodus.e: Exodus 15:1

Exodus.q: What was the name of the place with bitter water?
Exodus.e: Exodus 15:23

Exodus.q: What did Moses throw into the water to make it sweet?
Exodus.e: Exodus 15:25

Exodus.q: Where did the Israelites find 70 palm trees?
Exodus.e: Exodus 15:27

Exodus.q: What was the name of the place where Moses struck the rock for water?
Exodus.e: Exodus 17:7

Exodus.q: Who led the battle against the Amalekites?
Exodus.e: Exodus 17:9

Exodus.q: Who held up Moses' hands during the battle?
Exodus.e: Exodus 17:12

Exodus.q: Who was Moses' sister?
Exodus.e: Exodus 15:20

Exodus.q: Which tribe was set apart for the service of the Tabernacle?
Exodus.e: Exodus 32:26

Exodus.q: What was the first commandment?
Exodus.e: Exodus 20:3

Exodus.q: What was the fourth commandment?
Exodus.e: Exodus 20:8

Exodus.q: What was the fifth commandment?
Exodus.e: Exodus 20:12

Exodus.q: What was the tenth commandment?
Exodus.e: Exodus 20:17

Exodus.q: How many years old was Moses when he stood before Pharaoh?
Exodus.e: Exodus 7:7

Exodus.q: How many years old was Aaron when he stood before Pharaoh?
Exodus.e: Exodus 7:7

Exodus.q: Which plague did the magicians fail to replicate?
Exodus.e: Exodus 8:18

Exodus.q: What did the Israelites borrow from the Egyptians?
Exodus.e: Exodus 12:35

Exodus.q: What did Moses carry out of Egypt that belonged to Joseph?
Exodus.e: Exodus 13:19

Exodus.q: What was the seventh plague?
Exodus.e: Exodus 9:23

Exodus.q: What was the ninth plague?
Exodus.e: Exodus 10:22

Exodus.q: How long did the darkness last?
Exodus.e: Exodus 10:23

Exodus.q: What was the name of the assistant to Bezalel?
Exodus.e: Exodus 31:6

Exodus.q: What happened to Moses' face after talking with God?
Exodus.e: Exodus 34:29

Exodus.q: How many days was Moses on the mountain?
Exodus.e: Exodus 24:18

Exodus.q: What were the tablets of the Law made of?
Exodus.e: Exodus 31:18

Exodus.q: Who was the father of Gershom?
Exodus.e: Exodus 2:22

Exodus.e: Exodus 32:2

Judges.q: Who was the first judge of Israel?
Judges.e: Judges 3:9 — The Lord raised up a deliverer for the Israelites, Othniel son of Kenaz.

Judges.q: Who was the only female judge?
Judges.e: Judges 4:4 — Now Deborah, a prophet, the wife of Lappidoth, was leading Israel.

Judges.q: Which judge killed 600 Philistines with an oxgoad?
Judges.e: Judges 3:31 — After Ehud came Shamgar... who struck down six hundred Philistines with an oxgoad.

Judges.q: What was the name of the woman who killed the Canaanite general Sisera?
Judges.e: Judges 4:21 — But Jael, Heber’s wife... drove the tent peg through his temple into the ground.

Judges.q: How many men were in Gideon's final army?
Judges.e: Judges 7:7 — The Lord said to Gideon, 'With the three hundred men... I will save you.'

Judges.q: What sign did Gideon ask of God to confirm his call?
Judges.e: Judges 6:37 — 'I will place a wool fleece on the threshing floor.'

Judges.q: Which judge made a rash vow that led to the sacrifice of his daughter?
Judges.e: Judges 11:30-31 — Jephthah made a vow to the Lord... 'whatever comes out of the door of my house...'

Judges.q: Who was Samson's father?
Judges.e: Judges 13:2 — A certain man of Zorah, named Manoah...

Judges.q: What animal did Samson kill with his bare hands?
Judges.e: Judges 14:6 — The Spirit of the Lord came powerfully upon him so that he tore the lion apart.

Judges.q: What did Samson use to kill 1,000 Philistines?
Judges.e: Judges 15:15 — Finding a fresh jawbone of a donkey... he struck down a thousand men.

Judges.q: Who was the woman who betrayed Samson to the Philistines?
Judges.e: Judges 16:4-18 — Delilah... said to him, 'Tell me the secret of your great strength.'

Judges.q: How did Samson die?
o: He was killed by a sword
He was hung from a tree
He pulled down the pillars of a temple
Judges.e: Judges 16:30 — He pushed with all his might, and down came the temple.

Judges.q: Which judge was a left-handed man?
Judges.e: Judges 3:15 — The Lord gave them a deliverer—Ehud, a left-handed man.

Judges.q: Who was the commander of the Israelite army during Deborah's time?
Judges.e: Judges 4:6 — She sent for Barak son of Abinoam...

Judges.q: What did Gideon's 300 men carry in their hands when they attacked the Midianites?
Judges.e: Judges 7:16 — He placed trumpets and empty jars in the hands of all of them, with torches inside.

Judges.q: Who was the king of Moab that Ehud killed?
Judges.e: Judges 3:17 — Ehud presented the tribute to Eglon king of Moab.

Judges.q: Which judge was the son of a prostitute?
Judges.e: Judges 11:1 — Jephthah the Gileadite was a mighty warrior. His father was Gilead; his mother was a prostitute.

Judges.q: How many Philistines did Samson kill at his death?
o: More than he killed in his lifetime
Judges.e: Judges 16:30 — Thus he killed many more when he died than while he lived.

Judges.q: What was the secret of Samson's strength?
Judges.e: Judges 16:17 — 'No razor has ever been used on my head... I have been a Nazirite.'

Judges.q: Which judge was found threshing wheat in a winepress?
Judges.e: Judges 6:11 — Gideon was threshing wheat in a winepress to keep it from the Midianites.

Judges.q: Who was Gideon's son who killed his seventy brothers?
Judges.e: Judges 9:5 — He went to his father’s home at Ophrah and on one stone murdered his seventy brothers.

Judges.q: How did Abimelech die?
o: A woman dropped a millstone on his head
Judges.e: Judges 9:53 — A woman dropped an upper millstone on his head and cracked his skull.

Judges.q: What was the recurring theme in the book of Judges?
o: Everyone did what was right in their own eyes
Judges.e: Judges 21:25 — In those days Israel had no king; everyone did as they saw fit.

Judges.q: How many years was Samson a judge over Israel?
Judges.e: Judges 15:20 — Samson led Israel for twenty years in the days of the Philistines.

Judges.q: Who was the judge who had thirty sons who rode thirty donkeys?
Judges.e: Judges 10:4 — He had thirty sons, who rode thirty donkeys.

Judges.q: Who was the judge from Bethlehem who had thirty sons and thirty daughters?
Judges.e: Judges 12:8-9 — Ibzan of Bethlehem... had thirty sons and thirty daughters.

Judges.q: Which judge served for ten years and was buried in Aijalon?
Judges.e: Judges 12:11-12 — Elon the Zebulunite led Israel ten years.

Judges.q: Who was the judge who had forty sons and thirty grandsons who rode on seventy donkeys?
Judges.e: Judges 12:13-14 — Abdon son of Hillel... had forty sons and thirty grandsons, who rode on seventy donkeys.

Judges.q: What did Samson find in the carcass of the lion he had killed?
o: A swarm of bees and honey
Judges.e: Judges 14:8 — He turned aside to look at the lion's carcass, and in it he saw a swarm of bees and some honey.

Judges.q: What was the riddle Samson posed to the Philistines?
o: Out of the eater, something to eat; out of the strong, something sweet.
What is stronger than a lion?
Who can find a virtuous woman?
Judges.e: Judges 14:14 — He said to them, 'Out of the eater, something to eat; out of the strong, something sweet.'

Judges.q: How many foxes did Samson catch to burn the Philistines' fields?
Judges.e: Judges 15:4 — So he went out and caught three hundred foxes.

Judges.q: Which judge was from the tribe of Issachar and lived in Shamir?
Judges.e: Judges 10:1 — After the time of Abimelech, a man of Issachar named Tola... rose to save Israel.

Judges.q: Who was the father of Gideon?
What did the people of Israel worship after Gideon died?
Judges.e: Judges 8:33 — No sooner had Gideon died than the Israelites... set up Baal-Berith as their god.

Judges.q: What was the name of the valley where Delilah lived?
Judges.e: Judges 16:4 — Some time later, he fell in love with a woman in the Valley of Sorek whose name was Delilah.

Judges.q: How many times did Delilah try to bind Samson before he told her the truth?
Judges.e: Judges 16:6-15 — She tried with fresh thongs, new ropes, and weaving his hair before he told her the truth.

Judges.q: What was the name of the Philistine god in whose temple Samson was brought?
Judges.e: Judges 16:23 — Now the rulers of the Philistines assembled to offer a great sacrifice to Dagon their god.

Judges.q: What was the password used by the Gileadites to identify the Ephraimites?
Judges.e: Judges 12:6 — If he said, 'Sibboleth,' because he could not pronounce the word correctly...

Judges.q: Who was the king of the Canaanites during the time of Deborah?
Judges.e: Judges 4:2 — The Lord sold them into the hands of Jabin king of Canaan.

Judges.q: What was the name of the place where Gideon built an altar to the Lord?
Judges.e: Judges 6:24 — So Gideon built an altar to the Lord there and called it The Lord Is Peace.

Judges.q: How many years was Israel in the hands of the Philistines before Samson?
Judges.e: Judges 13:1 — Again the Israelites did evil... so the Lord delivered them into the hands of the Philistines for forty years.

Judges.q: Which judge had a mother who was a 'prophetess'? (Wait, actually Deborah was the prophetess/judge). Let's rephrase: Who was the judge associated with the city of Gilead?
What was the name of Gideon's other name?
Judges.e: Judges 6:32 — So because Gideon broke down Baal's altar, they gave him the name Jerub-Baal.

Judges.q: Which judge's name means 'Strong'? (Wait, Shamgar is often associated with that, but let's stick to facts). Who was the judge who led Israel after Tola?
Judges.e: Judges 10:3 — He was followed by Jair of Gilead, who led Israel twenty-two years.

Judges.q: How many years did the land have peace after Ehud?
Judges.e: Judges 3:30 — That day Moab was made subject to Israel, and the land had peace for eighty years.

Judges.q: What did the angel of the Lord do to the meat and bread Gideon prepared?
o: Touched it with a staff and fire consumed it
Told him to throw it away
Judges.e: Judges 6:21 — With the tip of the staff... the angel of the Lord touched the meat and the unleavened bread. Fire flared from the rock, consuming the meat and the bread.

Judges.q: Who was the only judge to have his birth announced by an angel?
Judges.e: Judges 13:3 — The angel of the Lord appeared to her and said... 'you will become pregnant and have a son.'

Judges.q: What did Jael give Sisera to drink?
Judges.e: Judges 4:19 — 'I’m thirsty,' he said. 'Please give me some water.' She opened a skin of milk and gave him a drink.

Judges.q: How many of Gideon's men left because they were afraid?
Judges.e: Judges 7:3 — Twenty-two thousand men left, while ten thousand remained.

Judges.q: What was the name of the place where the Israelites wept because the angel of the Lord rebuked them?
Judges.e: Judges 2:1-5 — They called that place Bokim (meaning 'weepers') and offered sacrifices there.

Kings.q: Who was the first king of Israel?
Kings.e: 1 Samuel 10:1 — Samuel anointed Saul as the first king of Israel.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel was known for his great wisdom?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 3:12 — God gave Solomon a wise and discerning heart.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel was the son of Nebat and made Israel to sin by setting up golden calves?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 12:28-30 — Jeroboam made two golden calves, one in Bethel and the other in Dan.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel was known for his furious driving of a chariot?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 9:20 — The driving is like that of Jehu son of Nimshi—he drives like a maniac.

Kings.q: Which king of Judah was the father of Hezekiah and was very wicked?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 16:1-2 — Ahaz did not do what was right in the eyes of the Lord.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Judah who sought the Lord and a great revival occurred, but he later made an alliance with Ahab?
Kings.e: 2 Chronicles 17:3-6 — The Lord was with Jehoshaphat because he walked in the ways of his father David.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Judah who was the son of Solomon and saw the kingdom split?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 12:1 — Rehoboam went to Shechem, for all Israel had gone there to make him king.

Kings.q: Which king of Judah removed his grandmother Maakah from her position because she made an Asherah pole?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 15:13 — King Asa also deposed his grandmother Maakah from her position as queen mother.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Israel who killed Pekahiah to take the throne?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 15:25 — Pekah son of Remaliah conspired against Pekahiah and killed him.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel was the father of Jeroboam II?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 14:23 — Jeroboam son of Jehoash king of Israel began to reign.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Israel that Elisha told to strike the ground with arrows?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 13:14-19 — Elisha told Joash (Jehoash) to strike the ground with the arrows.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Israel that died after falling through the lattice of his upper room?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 1:2 — Ahaziah fell through the lattice of his upper room in Samaria and was injured.

Kings.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Jehoshaphat and married a daughter of Ahab?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 8:16-18 — Jehoram son of Jehoshaphat became king... He walked in the ways of the kings of Israel, as the house of Ahab had done.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel was the son of Baasha and was killed by Zimri while he was getting drunk?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 16:8-10 — Zimri... struck down Elah and killed him while he was in Tirzah, getting drunk.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Judah who defeated the Edomites in the Valley of Salt but then turned to their gods?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 14:7 — He (Amaziah) was the one who defeated ten thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel was the son of Nadab and was killed by Baasha?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 15:27-28 — Baasha son of Ahijah... struck him (Nadab) down at Gibbethon.

Kings.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Josiah and was placed on the throne by Pharaoh Necho?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 23:34 — Pharaoh Necho made Eliakim son of Josiah king... and changed his name to Jehoiakim.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel was the son of Pekahiah?
o: Pekahiah was the son of Menahem
Kings.e: 2 Kings 15:22-23 — Menahem rested with his ancestors. And Pekahiah his son succeeded him as king.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Syria that Hazael murdered to take the throne?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 8:15 — The next day he (Hazael) took a thick cloth, soaked it in water and spread it over the king’s (Ben-Hadad) face, so that he died.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel was the son of Omri and built Samaria as the capital?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 16:24-28 — Omri... bought the hill of Samaria... and built a city on it. Ahab his son succeeded him.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Israel that Elisha anointed to destroy the house of Ahab?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 9:1-6 — A young man from the prophets... anointed Jehu king over the Lord’s people.

Kings.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Josiah and was killed in battle at Megiddo?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 23:29-30 — King Josiah marched out to meet him in battle, but Necho faced him and killed him at Megiddo.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Judah who was the son of Solomon?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 11:43 — Then Solomon rested with his ancestors... And Rehoboam his son succeeded him as king.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel committed suicide by setting his palace on fire around himself?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 16:18 — When Zimri saw that the city was taken, he went into the citadel of the royal palace and set the palace on fire around him.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Israel who was challenged for the throne by Tibni?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 16:21 — Then the people of Israel were split into two factions; half supported Tibni... and the other half supported Omri.

Kings.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Abijam (Abijah)?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 15:8 — And Asa his son succeeded him as king.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Judah who made a peace treaty with the king of Israel (Ahab)?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 22:44 — Jehoshaphat was also at peace with the king of Israel.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Judah who was the son of Jotham?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 15:38 — And Ahaz his son succeeded him as king.

Kings.q: Which king of Israel was the son of Gadi?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 15:14 — Menahem son of Gadi... struck down Shallum.

Kings.q: Who was the king of Israel who was the son of Jabesh?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 15:10 — Shallum son of Jabesh conspired against Zechariah.

Kings.q: Which Psalm says, 'Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile'?
Kings.e: Psalm 34:13 — Keep thy tongue from evil.

Kings.q: Which Psalm says, 'My heart is inditing a good matter: I speak of the things which I have made touching the king'?
Kings.e: Psalm 45:1 — My heart is inditing a good matter.

Kings.q: Who was the queen of Sheba who visited King Solomon?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 10:1 — When the queen of Sheba heard about the fame of Solomon.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Josiah?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 22:1 — His mother’s name was Jedidah daughter of Adaiah.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Hezekiah?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 18:2 — His mother’s name was Abi daughter of Zechariah.

Kings.q: Who was the mother of King Solomon's successor, Rehoboam?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 14:21 — His mother’s name was Naamah; she was an Ammonite.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King David's son, Absalom?
Kings.e: 2 Samuel 3:3 — The third [son] was Absalom the son of Maakah.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King David's son, Adonijah?
Kings.e: 2 Samuel 3:4 — The fourth, Adonijah the son of Haggith.

Kings.q: Who was the woman of Shunem who built a room for Elisha?
o: The woman with the issue of blood
Kings.e: 2 Kings 4:8-10 — A well-to-do woman... said to her husband, 'Let’s make a small room on the roof.'

Kings.q: Who was the widow of Zarephath who fed Elijah during the famine?
o: The woman with the issue of blood
Kings.e: 1 Kings 17:9-10 — I have directed a widow there to supply you with food.

Kings.q: Which woman was the sister of King Agrippa II?
Kings.e: Acts 25:13 — King Agrippa and Bernice arrived at Caesarea.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King David's son, Ithream?
Kings.e: 2 Samuel 3:5 — And the sixth [son was] Ithream the son of David’s wife Eglah.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King David's son, Shephatiah?
Kings.e: 2 Samuel 3:4 — The fifth, Shephatiah the son of Abital.

Kings.q: Who was the mother of King David's son, Amnon?
Kings.e: 2 Samuel 3:2 — His firstborn was Amnon the son of Ahinoam of Jezreel.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Solomon's son, Rehoboam?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 14:21 — His mother’s name was Naamah.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Abijah?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 15:2 — His mother’s name was Maakah daughter of Abishalom.

Kings.q: Who was the wife of King Josiah?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 23:31 and 23:36 — Jehoahaz's mother was Hamutal, and Jehoiakim's mother was Zebidah.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Jehoahaz?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 23:31 — His mother’s name was Hamutal.

Kings.q: Who was the mother of King Jehoiakim?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 23:36 — His mother’s name was Zebidah.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Jehoiachin?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 24:8 — His mother’s name was Nehushta.

Kings.q: Who was the mother of King Zedekiah?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 24:18 — His mother’s name was Hamutal.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Asa?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 15:10 — His grandmother’s name was Maakah daughter of Abishalom.

Kings.q: Who was the wife of King Jehoshaphat?
Kings.e: The Bible does not record the name of Jehoshaphat's wife.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Jehoram of Judah?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 22:42 — His mother’s name was Azubah daughter of Shilhi.

Kings.q: Who was the wife of King Jehoram of Judah and daughter of Ahab?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 8:18 — For he married a daughter of Ahab.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Ahaziah of Judah?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 8:26 — His mother’s name was Athaliah, a granddaughter of Omri.

Kings.q: Who was the wife of King Joash of Judah?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 12:1 — His mother’s name was Zibiah; she was from Beersheba.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Uzziah (Azariah)?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 15:2 — His mother’s name was Jecoliah.

Kings.q: Who was the wife of King Uzziah?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 15:33 — His (Jotham's) mother’s name was Jerusha daughter of Zadok.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Jotham?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 15:33 — His mother’s name was Jerusha.

Kings.q: Who was the wife of King Jotham?
Kings.e: The Bible does not record the name of Jotham's wife.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Ahaz?
Kings.e: The Bible does not record the name of Ahaz's mother.

Kings.q: Who was the wife of King Ahaz?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 18:2 — His (Hezekiah's) mother’s name was Abi.

Kings.q: Which woman was the mother of King Amon?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 21:19 — His mother’s name was Meshullemeth.

Kings.q: Who was the mother of King Josiah?
Kings.e: 2 Kings 22:1 — His mother’s name was Jedidah.

Kings.q: Which animal in Proverbs 30 can be caught with the hands, yet is in kings' palaces?
Kings.e: Proverbs 30:28 — A lizard can be caught with the hand, yet it is found in kings’ palaces.

Kings.q: What animal did Solomon import along with gold, silver, and ivory?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 10:22 — They brought gold, silver and ivory, and apes and baboons (or peacocks).

Kings.q: What animal did the Philistines send back with the Ark as a guilt offering?
Kings.e: 1 Samuel 6:4 — Five gold tumors and five gold rats.

Kings.q: Which animal is mentioned as being 'in kings' palaces' in Proverbs 30?
Kings.e: Proverbs 30:28 — A lizard... is found in kings’ palaces.

Kings.q: What city was the capital of the Northern Kingdom of Israel?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 16:24 — Omri... built a city on it, which he called Samaria.

Kings.q: Where did David hide from Saul in a cave?
Kings.e: 1 Samuel 22:1 (Adullam) and 1 Samuel 24:1 (En Gedi).

Kings.q: Which city was the home of Goliath?
Kings.e: 1 Samuel 17:4 — A champion named Goliath, who was from Gath, came out of the Philistine camp.

Kings.q: Where did the Queen of Sheba come from to visit Solomon?
Kings.e: 1 Kings 10:1 — When the queen of Sheba heard about the fame of Solomon. Traditionally identified with Ethiopia or Sheba.

Kings.q: In what city did the prophet Elisha live?
Kings.e: Elisha lived in the Northern Kingdom, often in Samaria.

Kings.q: Which city did the Lord tell the prophet Amos to prophecy against?
Kings.e: Amos was a prophet from Judah sent to the Northern Kingdom.

Kings.q: In what city did the Lord tell the prophet Hosea to live?
Kings.e: Hosea was a prophet to the Northern Kingdom (Israel).

Kings.q: Where did the Lord appear to Samuel as a child?
Kings.e: 1 Samuel 3:21 — The Lord continued to appear at Shiloh.

Kings.q: Who was the wife of Terah and mother of Abraham?
Kings.e: The Bible does not record the name of Terah's wife.

Kings.q: Which animal is described as having its strength in its loins and power in the muscles of its belly in Job 40?
Kings.e: Job 40:15-16 — Look at Behemoth... its strength is in its loins, its power in the muscles of its belly.

Kings.q: Which animal is called 'exceeding wise' in Proverbs 30 despite being small?
Kings.e: Proverbs 30:24-25 — Four things on earth are small, yet they are extremely wise: Ants are creatures of little strength, yet they store up their food in the summer.

Kings.q: What animal is the sluggard told to consider in Proverbs 6?
Kings.e: Proverbs 6:6 — Go to the ant, you sluggard; consider its ways and be wise!

Kings.q: Which bird did God tell Job 'treats her young harshly, as if they were not hers'?
Kings.e: Job 39:13-16 — The wings of the ostrich flap joyfully... She treats her young harshly.

Kings.q: Which animal is mentioned as being 'wise' in Proverbs 30?
Kings.e: Proverbs 30:24-25 — Ants are... extremely wise.

Kings.q: Which animal is mentioned as being 'little upon the earth' but wise in Proverbs 30?
Kings.e: Proverbs 30:26 — Hyraxes (Conies) are creatures of little power, yet they make their home in the crags.

Kings.q: Where did the Israelites cross the Red Sea?
Kings.e: The exact location of the 'Yam Suph' (Red Sea/Reed Sea) is a subject of scholarly debate.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm is the longest in the Bible?
Psalms.e: Psalm 119 has 176 verses.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm is the shortest in the Bible?
Psalms.e: Psalm 117 has only 2 verses.

Psalms.q: The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want' is the beginning of which Psalm?
Psalms.e: Psalm 23:1 — The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm begins with 'The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 19:1 — The heavens declare the glory of God.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 119:105 — Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'Make a joyful noise unto the Lord, all ye lands'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 100:1 — Make a joyful noise unto the Lord, all ye lands.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 91:1 — He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 121:1 — I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm begins with 'Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 1:1 — Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'This is the day which the Lord hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 118:24 — This is the day which the Lord hath made.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'Bless the Lord, O my soul: and all that is within me, bless his holy name'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 103:1 — Bless the Lord, O my soul.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm is often read at funerals and mentions the 'valley of the shadow of death'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 23:4 — Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?'
Psalms.e: Psalm 27:1 — The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 42:1 — As the hart panteth after the water brooks.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 139:23 — Search me, O God, and know my heart.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 14:1 — The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble'?
Psalms.e: Psalm 46:1 — God is our refuge and strength.

Psalms.q: Which Psalm says, 'My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?' (also quoted by Jesus on the cross)
Psalms.e: Psalm 22:1 — My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?

Who Am I?.q: I was a tax collector who climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus. Who am I?

Who Am I?.q: I was a shepherd boy who defeated a giant named Goliath. Who am I?

Who Am I?.q: I was the wisest king of Israel and built the Temple in Jerusalem. Who am I?

Who Am I?.q: I was the husband of Mary and a carpenter by trade. Who am I?
