Miracles.q: What was Jesus' first miracle?
Miracles.e: John 2:11 — This, the first of his signs, Jesus performed at Cana in Galilee.

Miracles.q: How many people did Jesus feed with five loaves and two fish?
Miracles.e: Matthew 14:21 — The number of those who ate was about five thousand men, besides women and children.

Miracles.q: Who walked on water with Jesus?
Miracles.e: Matthew 14:29 — Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water and came toward Jesus.

Miracles.q: How many people were raised from the dead in the Bible?
Miracles.e: Various — Elijah, Elisha, Jesus, Peter, and Paul all performed or were involved in resurrections.

Miracles.q: Which miracle did Elisha perform for the widow?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 4:1-7 — Elisha multiplied the widow's oil to pay her debts.

Miracles.q: What happened when Jesus touched the ear of the high priest's servant?
Miracles.e: Luke 22:51 — And he touched his ear and healed him.

Miracles.q: How many years had the woman with the issue of blood been suffering?
Miracles.e: Luke 8:43 — And a woman was there who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years.

Miracles.q: Who was raised from the dead after being in the tomb for four days?
Miracles.e: John 11:39 — 'By this time there is a bad odor, for he has been there four days.'

Miracles.q: What did Jesus use to heal the blind man at Bethsaida?
Miracles.e: Mark 8:23 — He spit on the man’s eyes and put his hands on him.

Miracles.q: How many lepers did Jesus heal at once?
Miracles.e: Luke 17:12-14 — Ten men who had leprosy met him.

Miracles.q: How many of the ten healed lepers returned to thank Jesus?
Miracles.e: Luke 17:15-18 — One of them, when he saw he was healed, came back...

Miracles.q: What did Peter find in the mouth of a fish to pay the temple tax?
Miracles.e: Matthew 17:27 — Take the first fish you catch; open its mouth and you will find a four-drachma coin.

Miracles.q: Whose mother-in-law did Jesus heal of a fever?
Miracles.e: Matthew 8:14-15 — Jesus saw Peter’s mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever... she got up and began to wait on him.

Miracles.q: What did Jesus say to the storm on the Sea of Galilee?
Miracles.e: Mark 4:39 — He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves, 'Quiet! Be still!'

Miracles.q: How many baskets of leftovers were collected after feeding the 5,000?
Miracles.e: Matthew 14:20 — They picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.

Miracles.q: Which prophet called down fire from heaven to consume a sacrifice?
Miracles.e: 1 Kings 18:38 — Then the fire of the Lord fell and burned up the sacrifice.

Miracles.q: What happened to the sun during the battle at Gibeon?
Miracles.e: Joshua 10:13 — The sun stopped in the middle of the sky and delayed going down about a full day.

Miracles.q: What miracle did Peter perform on Aeneas?
Miracles.e: Acts 9:33-34 — 'Aeneas,' Peter said to him, 'Jesus Christ heals you. Get up.'

Miracles.q: Who was restored to life by Peter in Joppa?
Miracles.e: Acts 9:40 — Peter sent them all out of the room; then he got down on his knees and prayed. Turning toward the dead woman, he said, 'Tabitha, get up.'

Miracles.q: How did Paul survive the bite of a poisonous viper on Malta?
o: He shook it off into the fire and suffered no ill effects
Miracles.e: Acts 28:5 — But Paul shook the snake off into the fire and suffered no ill effects.

Miracles.q: What miracle happened at the pool of Bethesda?
o: An angel stirred the water for healing
Miracles.e: John 5:4 — From time to time an angel of the Lord would come down and stir up the waters.

Miracles.q: Who was healed of leprosy after washing in the Jordan River seven times?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 5:14 — So he went down and dipped himself in the Jordan seven times... and his flesh was restored.

Miracles.q: What did Elisha do to make the iron ax head float?
o: He threw a stick into the water
He touched it with his staff
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 6:6 — Elisha cut a stick and threw it there, and made the iron float.

Miracles.q: What miracle did Jesus perform for the Centurion's servant?
Miracles.e: Matthew 8:13 — Then Jesus said to the centurion, 'Go! Let it be done just as you believed it would.' And his servant was healed at that moment.

Miracles.q: What was the name of the man Jesus healed of many demons, who lived in the tombs?
Miracles.e: Mark 5:9 — Then Jesus asked him, 'What is your name?' 'My name is Legion,' he replied, 'for we are many.'

Miracles.q: How many people were fed with seven loaves and a few small fish?
Miracles.e: Matthew 15:38 — The number of those who ate was four thousand men, besides women and children.

Miracles.q: Which miracle did Jesus perform on the Sabbath that angered the Pharisees?
o: Healed a man with a shriveled hand
Miracles.e: Matthew 12:10-13 — Then he said to the man, 'Stretch out your hand.' He stretched it out and it was completely restored.

Miracles.q: How did Jesus heal the man born blind in John 9?
o: Spit on the ground, made mud, and put it on his eyes
Miracles.e: John 9:6 — He spit on the ground, made some mud with the saliva, and put it on the man’s eyes.

Miracles.q: What did the disciples think they saw when Jesus was walking on the water?
Miracles.e: Matthew 14:26 — When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified. 'It’s a ghost,' they said.

Miracles.q: Whose ear was cut off and then healed by Jesus in the garden?
Miracles.e: John 18:10 — The servant’s name was Malchus. (Luke 22:51 mentions the healing).

Miracles.q: What did Elisha do to the Shunammite woman's son?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 4:32-35 — Elisha went in, shut the door... and the boy sneezed seven times and opened his eyes.

Miracles.q: How was the shadow on the sundial moved back ten steps for King Hezekiah?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 20:11 — Then the prophet Isaiah called on the Lord, and the Lord made the shadow go back.

Miracles.q: What miracle happened when the Israelites carried the Ark into the Jordan River?
Miracles.e: Joshua 3:15-16 — As soon as the priests who carried the ark reached the Jordan... the water from upstream stopped flowing.

Miracles.q: How did God provide water for the Israelites in the desert at Rephidim?
Miracles.e: Exodus 17:6 — Strike the rock, and water will come out of it for the people to drink.

Miracles.q: What miracle did Paul perform in Lystra?
o: Healed a man lame from birth
Raised a man from the dead
Miracles.e: Acts 14:8-10 — Paul looked at him... and called out, 'Stand up on your feet!' At that, the man jumped up and began to walk.

Miracles.q: Who was blinded on the road to Damascus by a great light?
Miracles.e: Acts 9:3-9 — A light from heaven flashed around him... For three days he was blind.

Miracles.q: What miracle happened to the prison doors while Paul and Silas were singing?
o: They were opened by an angel
Miracles.e: Acts 16:26 — Suddenly there was such a violent earthquake that the foundations of the prison were shaken. At once all the prison doors flew open.

Miracles.q: How did Elisha heal the poisonous stew?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 4:41 — Elisha said, 'Get some flour.' He put it into the pot... and there was nothing harmful in the pot.

Miracles.q: What did the prophet Isaiah use to heal King Hezekiah's boil?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 20:7 — Then Isaiah said, 'Prepare a poultice of figs.' They did so and applied it to the boil, and he recovered.

Miracles.q: What miracle did Elisha perform on the waters of Jericho?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 2:21 — Then he went out to the spring and threw salt into it... 'I have healed this water.'

Miracles.q: How many times did Elijah stretch himself over the widow's son to raise him?
Miracles.e: 1 Kings 17:21 — Then he stretched himself out on the boy three times and cried out to the Lord.

Miracles.q: What happened to the Egyptian chariots in the Red Sea before the water fell?
Miracles.e: Exodus 14:25 — He jammed the wheels of their chariots so that they had difficulty driving.

Miracles.q: Who was healed of leprosy after questioning Moses' authority?
Miracles.e: Numbers 12:10 — When the cloud lifted from above the tent, Miriam's skin was leprous.

Miracles.q: What miracle did God perform to show He had chosen Aaron?
o: His staff budded and produced almonds
Miracles.e: Numbers 17:8 — The next day Moses entered the tent... and Aaron’s staff... had not only sprouted but had budded, blossomed and produced almonds.

Miracles.q: How was the wall of Jericho destroyed?
o: The people shouted and the wall fell
Miracles.e: Joshua 6:20 — When the trumpets sounded, the army shouted, and at the sound of the trumpet, when the men gave a loud shout, the wall collapsed.

Miracles.q: What miracle happened when the dead man's body touched Elisha's bones?
o: The man came back to life
The man was healed of leprosy
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 13:21 — When the body touched Elisha’s bones, the man came to life and stood up on his feet.

Miracles.q: How did Jesus heal the man lowered through the roof?
o: Said his sins were forgiven and told him to walk
Miracles.e: Mark 2:5-12 — 'Son, your sins are forgiven... Get up, take your mat and go home.'

Miracles.q: What was the result of Jesus cursing the fig tree?
Miracles.e: Matthew 21:19 — Immediately the tree withered.

Miracles.q: Who was healed of blindness after Paul prayed for him in Malta?
Miracles.e: Acts 28:8 — His father was sick in bed... Paul went in to see him and, after prayer, placed his hands on him and healed him.

Miracles.q: What miracle occurred when Peter and John met the lame man at the Beautiful Gate?
o: He was healed and began to walk and leap
He was given a new mat
He was raised from the dead
Miracles.e: Acts 3:6-8 — 'In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk.'... He jumped to his feet and began to walk.

Miracles.q: Which king of Judah saw the sun's shadow go backward ten degrees as a sign of healing?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 20:11 — The shadow went back the ten steps it had gone down on the stairway of Ahaz.

Miracles.q: Who was the king of Syria who was healed of leprosy by Elisha?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 5:1 — Naaman was commander of the army of the king of Aram (Syria).

Miracles.q: Who was the sister of Mary and Lazarus?
Miracles.e: John 11:1 — Mary and her sister Martha lived in Bethany.

Miracles.q: Which woman was known for her good works and acts of charity, and was raised from the dead by Peter?
Miracles.e: Acts 9:36 — In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha (which is translated as Dorcas).

Miracles.q: Who was the queen mother who tried to destroy the entire royal family of Judah?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 11:1 — When Athaliah the mother of Ahaziah saw that her son was dead, she proceeded to destroy the whole royal family.

Miracles.q: Which woman was the wife of Ananias and was struck dead for lying?
Miracles.e: Acts 5:1 — Now a man named Ananias, together with his wife Sapphira.

Miracles.q: Who was the woman who touched the hem of Jesus' garment and was healed?
o: The woman with the issue of blood
Miracles.e: Matthew 9:20 — A woman who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years.

Miracles.q: What animal did the Israelites make a representation of to look at and be healed?
Miracles.e: Numbers 21:8 — The Lord said to Moses, 'Make a snake and put it up on a pole.'

Miracles.q: What is the lowest point on earth mentioned in the Bible (and in reality)?
Miracles.e: The Salt Sea (Dead Sea) is frequently mentioned in the Old Testament (e.g., Genesis 14:3).

Miracles.q: Which city did Jesus perform his first miracle at a wedding?
Miracles.e: John 2:11 — What Jesus did here in Cana of Galilee was the first of the signs through which he revealed his glory.

Miracles.q: Where was the home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus?
Miracles.e: John 11:1 — Now a man named Lazarus was sick. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.

Miracles.q: Which city did Jesus lament over, saying he wanted to gather its children as a hen gathers her chicks?
Miracles.e: Matthew 23:37 — Jerusalem, Jerusalem... how often I have longed to gather your children together.

Miracles.q: In what city did Paul raise Eutychus from the dead after he fell out of a window?
Miracles.e: Acts 20:5-12 — We sailed from Philippi... and joined the others at Troas.

Miracles.q: In what city did Peter raise Dorcas (Tabitha) from the dead?
Miracles.e: Acts 9:36-42 — In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha... Peter went with them.

Miracles.q: Where did the Lord appear to Moses in the burning bush?
Miracles.e: Exodus 3:1-2 — He came to Horeb... There the angel of the Lord appeared to him in flames of fire from within a bush.

Miracles.q: How many days was Lazarus in the tomb before Jesus raised him?
Miracles.e: John 11:17 — On his arrival, Jesus found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.

Miracles.q: How many times did Naaman wash in the Jordan to be healed of leprosy?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 5:14 — So he went down and dipped himself in the Jordan seven times.

Miracles.q: What was the law regarding the High Priest and a dead body?
o: He could only touch his parents' bodies
He must not make himself unclean for any dead person, even his father or mother
He was the only one allowed to touch the dead
Miracles.e: Leviticus 21:11 — He must not enter a place where there is a dead body. He must not make himself unclean, even for his father or mother.

Miracles.q: What was the law regarding the 'Nazirite's Vow' and dead bodies?
o: They must not go near a dead body, even that of their father or mother
They were the only ones allowed to bury the dead
Miracles.e: Numbers 6:6-7 — Throughout the period of their dedication to the Lord, they must not go near a dead body.

Miracles.q: Who was raised from the dead after 4 days?
Miracles.e: John 11

Miracles.q: How many lepers did Jesus heal at once?
Miracles.e: Luke 17:12

Miracles.q: What did Jesus use to heal the blind man's eyes?
Miracles.e: John 9:6

Miracles.q: How many people did Jesus feed with 5 loaves and 2 fish?
Miracles.e: Matthew 14:21

Miracles.q: Who walked on water with Jesus?
Miracles.e: Matthew 14:29

Miracles.q: What was Jesus' first miracle?
Miracles.e: John 2:11

Miracles.q: What did Jesus say to calm the storm?
Miracles.e: Mark 4:39

Miracles.q: Whose mother-in-law did Jesus heal of a fever?
Miracles.e: Matthew 8:14

Miracles.q: How many years had the woman with the issue of blood suffered?
Miracles.e: Mark 5:25

Miracles.q: What was the name of the man Jesus healed of many demons?
Miracles.e: Mark 5:9

Miracles.q: What was the miracle at the pool of Bethesda?
o: Healed a man lame for 38 years
Miracles.e: John 5:5

Miracles.q: Who was lowered through a roof to be healed by Jesus?
Miracles.e: Mark 2:4

Miracles.q: What happened to the ear of the high priest's servant?
o: Jesus healed it after Peter cut it off
Miracles.e: Luke 22:51

Miracles.q: What was the miracle for the widow of Nain?
o: Jesus raised her son from the dead
Miracles.e: Luke 7:14

Miracles.q: How many baskets were left after feeding the 5000?
Miracles.e: Matthew 14:20

Miracles.q: How many people were fed in the second multiplication of loaves?
Miracles.e: Matthew 15:38

Miracles.q: How many baskets were left after feeding the 4000?
Miracles.e: Matthew 15:37

Miracles.q: What did Jesus find in the mouth of a fish?
Miracles.e: Matthew 17:27

Miracles.q: What happened to the fig tree Jesus cursed?
Miracles.e: Matthew 21:19

Miracles.q: Which prophet called down fire from heaven?
Miracles.e: 1 Kings 18

Miracles.q: Which prophet parted the Jordan with his cloak?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 2:8

Miracles.q: Which prophet multiplied a widow's oil?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 4:1-7

Miracles.q: Who was healed of leprosy after washing in the Jordan?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 5:14

Miracles.q: What floated in the water at Elisha's command?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 6:6

Miracles.q: Whose bones raised a dead man back to life?
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 13:21

Miracles.q: What was the miracle of the sun for Hezekiah?
o: The shadow went back ten steps
Miracles.e: 2 Kings 20:11

Miracles.q: Who was raised from the dead by Peter?
Miracles.e: Acts 9:40

Miracles.q: Who fell out of a window and was raised by Paul?
What miracle happened at the gate called Beautiful?
o: A lame man was healed by Peter
Miracles.e: Acts 3:1-8

Miracles.q: What happened when Paul was bitten by a viper?
Miracles.e: Acts 28:3-5

Miracles.q: What was the miracle for the blind man at Bethsaida?
o: Jesus healed him in two stages

Miracles.q: What did Jesus do for the daughter of the Syrophoenician woman?
o: Healed her of a demon from a distance

Miracles.q: What happened to the shadow of Peter?
Miracles.e: Acts 5:15

Miracles.q: What happened to the handkerchiefs touched by Paul?
Miracles.e: Acts 19:12

Miracles.q: What was the miracle for the official's son at Capernaum?
o: Jesus healed him from a distance
Miracles.e: John 4:50

Miracles.q: What was the miracle of the great catch of fish (Luke 5)?
Miracles.e: Luke 5:6

Women of the Bible.q: Which king was humbled by God and lived like an animal for seven years?
Women of the Bible.e: Daniel 4:33 — Nebuchadnezzar was driven from men and ate grass like oxen.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the king of Israel who reigned for only seven days?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Kings 16:15 — Zimri reigned in Tirzah seven days.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the king of Israel who married Jezebel?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Kings 16:30-31 — Ahab son of Omri married Jezebel daughter of Ethbaal king of the Sidonians.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the king of Israel who killed all the descendants of Ahab?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Kings 10:11 — So Jehu killed everyone in Jezreel who remained of the house of Ahab.

Women of the Bible.q: Which king of Judah was the most wicked, even sacrificing his own children, but later repented in Babylon?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Chronicles 33:1-13 — Manasseh did evil in the eyes of the Lord but later humbled himself greatly.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the king of Israel who reigned for 12 years and did evil, but not as much as his parents Ahab and Jezebel?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Kings 3:1-2 — Joram (Jehoram) son of Ahab became king of Israel... He did evil in the eyes of the Lord, but not as his father and mother had done.

Women of the Bible.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Jotham and sacrificed his son in the fire?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Kings 16:1-3 — Ahaz son of Jotham became king... He even sacrificed his son in the fire.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the king of Judah who was the son of Amaziah and was also called Azariah?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Kings 15:1 — In the twenty-seventh year of Jeroboam king of Israel, Azariah (Uzziah) son of Amaziah king of Judah began to reign.

Women of the Bible.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Josiah and reigned for 11 years?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Kings 23:36 — Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem eleven years.

Women of the Bible.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Josiah and reigned for 11 years before Jerusalem fell?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Kings 24:18 — Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem eleven years.

Women of the Bible.q: Which Psalm is known as the 'Psalm of Repentance' written by David after his sin with Bathsheba?
Women of the Bible.e: Psalm 51 — Have mercy on me, O God, according to your unfailing love.

Women of the Bible.q: Which Psalm is a song of praise that ends with 'Let every thing that hath breath praise the Lord'?
Women of the Bible.e: Psalm 150:6 — Let every thing that hath breath praise the Lord.

Women of the Bible.q: Which Psalm says, 'O give thanks unto the Lord; for he is good: because his mercy endureth for ever'?
Women of the Bible.e: Psalm 136 repeats 'for his mercy endureth for ever' in every verse.

Women of the Bible.q: Which Psalm says, 'O send out thy light and thy truth: let them lead me; let them bring me unto thy holy hill'?
Women of the Bible.e: Psalm 43:3 — O send out thy light and thy truth.

Women of the Bible.q: Which Psalm says, 'The mighty God, even the Lord, hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof'?
Women of the Bible.e: Psalm 50:1 — The mighty God, even the Lord, hath spoken.

Women of the Bible.q: Which Psalm says, 'For every beast of the forest is mine, and the cattle upon a thousand hills'?
Women of the Bible.e: Psalm 50:10 — For every beast of the forest is mine.

Women of the Bible.q: Which Psalm says, 'For I acknowledge my transgressions: and my sin is ever before me'?
Women of the Bible.e: Psalm 51:3 — For I acknowledge my transgressions.

Women of the Bible.q: Which Psalm says, 'Evening, and morning, and at noon, will I pray, and cry aloud: and he shall hear my voice'?
Women of the Bible.e: Psalm 55:17 — Evening, and morning, and at noon.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the first woman mentioned in the Bible?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 2:21-23 — Eve was the first woman.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was a judge and a prophetess of Israel?
Women of the Bible.e: Judges 4:4 — Deborah, a prophetess, the wife of Lappidoth, was leading Israel at that time.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the mother of Samuel?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Samuel 1:20 — Hannah gave birth to a son and named him Samuel.

Women of the Bible.q: Which queen saved her people from a plot to destroy them?
Women of the Bible.e: The Book of Esther tells how she saved the Jews in Persia.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the mother of John the Baptist?
Women of the Bible.e: Luke 1:13 — Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you are to call him John.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the sister of Moses and Aaron?
Women of the Bible.e: Exodus 15:20 — Then Miriam the prophet, Aaron’s sister, took a timbrel.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the first person to see the risen Jesus?
Women of the Bible.e: John 20:14-16 — Jesus appeared first to Mary Magdalene.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was a moabitess who became the great-grandmother of King David?
Women of the Bible.e: Ruth 4:17 — They named him Obed. He was the father of Jesse, the father of David.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of Isaac and mother of Jacob and Esau?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 24:67 — Isaac married Rebekah and she became his wife.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman betrayed Samson by cutting his hair?
Women of the Bible.e: Judges 16:18-19 — Delilah had a man shave off the seven braids of Samson's hair.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was a seller of purple cloth and the first convert in Philippi?
Women of the Bible.e: Acts 16:14 — Lydia was a dealer in purple cloth from the city of Thyatira.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of Zechariah and mother of John the Baptist?
Women of the Bible.e: Luke 1:5 — His wife was also a descendant of Aaron; her name was Elizabeth.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 17:19 — Your wife Sarah will bear you a son, and you will call him Isaac.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was a prostitute in Jericho who hid the two spies?
Women of the Bible.e: Joshua 2:1 — They went and entered the house of a prostitute named Rahab.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the daughter of Jacob and Leah?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 30:21 — Later she gave birth to a daughter and named her Dinah.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the wife of King Ahab and was notorious for her wickedness?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Kings 16:31 — Ahab married Jezebel daughter of Ethbaal king of the Sidonians.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of David and daughter of Saul?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Samuel 18:27 — Saul gave him his daughter Michal in marriage.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the mother of King Solomon?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Samuel 12:24 — Bathsheba gave birth to a son, and they named him Solomon.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the mother-in-law of Ruth?
Women of the Bible.e: Ruth 1:3 — Elimelech, Naomi’s husband, died, and she was left with her two sons.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was a deaconess in the church at Cenchreae?
Women of the Bible.e: Romans 16:1 — I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a deacon of the church in Cenchreae.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of Aquila and a fellow worker with Paul?
Women of the Bible.e: Acts 18:2 — He met a Jew named Aquila... with his wife Priscilla.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of Nabal and later King David?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Samuel 25:3 — The man’s name was Nabal and his wife’s name was Abigail.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of Jacob and mother of Joseph and Benjamin?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 30:22-24 — God listened to Rachel... she gave birth to a son and said, 'God has taken away my disgrace.'

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the older sister of Rachel and first wife of Jacob?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 29:16 — Laban had two daughters; the name of the older was Leah.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the daughter of Saul and wife of Palti son of Laish?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Samuel 25:44 — Saul had given his daughter Michal... to Palti son of Laish.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the sister of Mary and Martha?
Women of the Bible.e: Luke 10:38-39 — A woman named Martha opened her home... She had a sister called Mary.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the mother of Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 29:32-35 — Leah became pregnant and gave birth to a son...

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of Elkanah and mother of Samuel?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Samuel 1:1-2 — He had two wives; one was called Hannah and the other Peninnah.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the mother of Cain and Abel?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 4:1-2 — Adam made love to his wife Eve, and she became pregnant and gave birth to Cain... Later she gave birth to his brother Abel.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the daughter of Laban and Jacob's favorite wife?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 29:18 — Jacob was in love with Rachel.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of King David who reproached him for dancing before the Ark?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Samuel 6:16 — Michal daughter of Saul... watched from a window... she despised him in her heart.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the mother of King David's son, Solomon?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Samuel 12:24 — Then David comforted his wife Bathsheba... she gave birth to a son, and they named him Solomon.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the mother of King David's son, Kileab (Daniel)?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Samuel 3:3 — His second, Kileab the son of Abigail.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the daughter of Laban and younger sister of Leah?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 29:16 — The name of the younger was Rachel.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the daughter of Bethuel and sister of Laban?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 24:29 — Rebekah had a brother named Laban.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the mother of Issachar and Zebulun?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 30:17-20 — Leah... gave birth to a fifth son... and a sixth son.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the woman who had seven demons cast out of her?
Women of the Bible.e: Luke 8:2 — Mary (called Magdalene) from whom seven demons had come out.

Women of the Bible.q: Which woman was the mother of James and Joseph (Joses)?
Women of the Bible.e: Matthew 27:56 — Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph.

Women of the Bible.q: Who was the wife of Lapidoth?
Women of the Bible.e: Judges 4:4 — Deborah, a prophetess, the wife of Lappidoth.

Women of the Bible.q: Which animals were used to carry the Ark of the Covenant when it was returned by the Philistines?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Samuel 6:7 — Get a new cart ready, with two cows that have calved and have never been yoked.

Women of the Bible.q: Elijah was fed by which animals while hiding at the Kerith Ravine?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Kings 17:4-6 — The ravens brought him bread and meat in the morning and bread and meat in the evening.

Women of the Bible.q: What animal's skin was used to make the first clothes for Adam and Eve?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 3:21 — The Lord God made garments of skin for Adam and his wife and clothed them.

Women of the Bible.q: What insects were the eighth plague on Egypt?
Women of the Bible.e: Exodus 10:12 — So that locusts settle over Egypt and devour everything growing in the fields.

Women of the Bible.q: What animal did the Israelites eat in the wilderness along with manna?
Women of the Bible.e: Exodus 16:13 — That evening quail came and covered the camp.

Women of the Bible.q: How many of each 'clean' animal did Noah take on the ark?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 7:2 — Take with you seven pairs of every kind of clean animal.

Women of the Bible.q: Which animal's fat was never to be eaten by the Israelites?
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 7:23 — Do not eat any of the fat of cattle, sheep or goats.

Women of the Bible.q: Which animal is mentioned as being able to 'understand the time of her coming' in Jeremiah 8?
Women of the Bible.e: Jeremiah 8:7 — Even the stork in the sky knows her appointed seasons, and the dove, the swift and the thrush observe the time of their migration.

Women of the Bible.q: Which animal is described as 'king over all that are proud' in Job 41?
Women of the Bible.e: Job 41:34 — He looks on every haughty thing; he is king over all that are proud. (Referring to Leviathan)

Women of the Bible.q: Which animal is mentioned in Revelation 4 as one of the four living creatures?
Women of the Bible.e: Revelation 4:7 — The first living creature was like a lion, the second was like an ox, the third had a face like a man, the fourth was like a flying eagle.

Women of the Bible.q: Which city's walls fell down after the Israelites marched around it?
Women of the Bible.e: Joshua 6:20 — The wall collapsed; so every man charged straight in, and they took the city.

Women of the Bible.q: Where did Jonah try to flee to when he was supposed to go to Nineveh?
Women of the Bible.e: Jonah 1:3 — But Jonah ran away from the Lord and headed for Tarshish.

Women of the Bible.q: Which island was John on when he received the Revelation?
Women of the Bible.e: Revelation 1:9 — I... was on the island of Patmos because of the word of God.

Women of the Bible.q: In what city did Paul see an altar 'To an Unknown God'?
Women of the Bible.e: Acts 17:22-23 — Men of Athens... I even found an altar with this inscription: TO AN UNKNOWN GOD.

Women of the Bible.q: Where did Elijah go to hide from Jezebel after the contest on Mount Carmel?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Kings 19:8 — He traveled forty days and forty nights until he reached Horeb, the mountain of God.

Women of the Bible.q: Which valley is known as the site of the future battle of Armageddon?
Women of the Bible.e: Revelation 16:16 — Then they gathered the kings together to the place that in Hebrew is called Armageddon.

Women of the Bible.q: In what city were the believers first called Christians?
Women of the Bible.e: Acts 11:26 — The disciples were called Christians first at Antioch.

Women of the Bible.q: Which city was the setting for the book of Ruth?
Women of the Bible.e: The story starts in Bethlehem, goes to Moab, and returns to Bethlehem.

Women of the Bible.q: Where did the Lord tell Jonah to go originally?
Women of the Bible.e: Jonah 1:2 — Go to the great city of Nineveh and preach against it.

Women of the Bible.q: Which city was the home of Rahab?
Women of the Bible.e: Joshua 2:1 — They went and entered the house of a prostitute named Rahab.

Women of the Bible.q: Where did the Lord tell the prophet Nahum to prophecy against?
Women of the Bible.e: The book of Nahum is a prophecy against Nineveh.

Women of the Bible.q: In what city did David first reign as king over Judah for seven years?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Samuel 2:11 — The length of time David was king in Hebron over Judah was seven years and six months.

Women of the Bible.q: Which city was the home of Mary and Martha?
Women of the Bible.e: John 11:1 — He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.

Women of the Bible.q: In what city did the Lord tell the prophet Jonah to go to?
Women of the Bible.e: Jonah 1:2 — Go to the great city of Nineveh.

Women of the Bible.q: On which day did God rest from His creation?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 2:2 — By the seventh day God had finished the work he had been doing; so on the seventh day he rested.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years did the Jews remain in Babylonian captivity?
Women of the Bible.e: Jeremiah 25:11 — These nations will serve the king of Babylon seventy years.

Women of the Bible.q: How many elders were around the throne in Revelation 4?
Women of the Bible.e: Revelation 4:4 — Surrounding the throne were twenty-four other thrones, and seated on them were twenty-four elders.

Women of the Bible.q: How many times did Joshua and the Israelites march around Jericho on the seventh day?
Women of the Bible.e: Joshua 6:4 — On the seventh day, march around the city seven times.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years did Jacob serve Laban for Rachel?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 29:18-30 — Jacob served seven years for Rachel, but was given Leah; he served another seven for Rachel.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years was Sarah when she gave birth to Isaac?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 17:17 — Will a woman of ninety bear a child?

Women of the Bible.q: How many wives did Solomon have?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Kings 11:3 — He had seven hundred wives of royal birth and three hundred concubines.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years was the Temple of Solomon in building?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Kings 6:38 — So he was seven years in building it.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years did Solomon take to build his own palace?
Women of the Bible.e: 1 Kings 7:1 — It took Solomon thirteen years, however, to complete the construction of his palace.

Women of the Bible.q: How many times should we forgive according to Jesus (Matt 18)?
Women of the Bible.e: Matthew 18:22 — Jesus answered, 'I tell you, not seven times, but seventy-seven times.' (KJV: seventy times seven)

Women of the Bible.q: How many deacons were chosen in Acts 6?
Women of the Bible.e: Acts 6:3 — Choose seven men from among you who are known to be full of the Spirit and wisdom.

Women of the Bible.q: How many souls were in the household of Jacob that came into Egypt?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 46:27 — All the members of Jacob’s family who went to Egypt were seventy in all.

Women of the Bible.q: How many days did Esther and the Jews fast before she went to the king?
Women of the Bible.e: Esther 4:16 — Do not eat or drink for three days, night or day.

Women of the Bible.q: How many sons did Haman have?
Women of the Bible.e: Esther 9:10 — The ten sons of Haman.

Women of the Bible.q: How many levels were on Noah's ark?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 6:16 — Put a door in the side of the ark and make lower, middle and upper decks.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years was Isaac when he married Rebekah?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 25:20 — Isaac was forty years old when he married Rebekah.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years was Isaac when Jacob and Esau were born?
Women of the Bible.e: Genesis 25:26 — Isaac was sixty years old when Rebekah gave birth to them.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years did Jabin oppress Israel (before Deborah)?
Women of the Bible.e: Judges 4:3 — He had oppressed the Israelites cruelly for twenty years.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years did the land have rest after Deborah?
Women of the Bible.e: Judges 5:31 — Then the land had peace forty years.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years did Midian oppress Israel (before Gideon)?
Women of the Bible.e: Judges 6:1 — For seven years he gave them into the hands of the Midianites.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years did Ibzan judge Israel?
Women of the Bible.e: Judges 12:9 — Ibzan led Israel seven years.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years was David in Hebron?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Samuel 5:5 — In Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months.

Women of the Bible.q: How many people died in the plague after David's census?
Women of the Bible.e: 2 Samuel 24:15 — Seventy thousand of the people from Dan to Beersheba died.

Women of the Bible.q: According to the Law, what should happen to a field during the seventh year (Sabbath Year)?
o: It should be planted with twice as much
It should be given to the poor
Women of the Bible.e: Exodus 23:11 — But during the seventh year let the land lie unplowed and unused.

Women of the Bible.q: According to the Law, how many years must a Hebrew slave serve before being set free?
Women of the Bible.e: Exodus 21:2 — If you buy a Hebrew servant, he is to serve you for six years. But in the seventh year, he shall go free.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the primary purpose of the Cities of Refuge?
o: For people who accidentally killed someone to flee to
Women of the Bible.e: Numbers 35:11 — Select some towns to be your cities of refuge, to which a person who has killed someone accidentally may flee.

Women of the Bible.q: How often were the Israelites to gather to hear the Law read publicly?
o: Every 7 years during the Festival of Tabernacles
Women of the Bible.e: Deuteronomy 31:10-11 — At the end of every seven years... during the Festival of Tabernacles... read this law before them.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding a man who raped an unengaged virgin?
o: He must be put to death
He must pay her father and marry her, and could never divorce her
He must pay a fine and the girl is free
Women of the Bible.e: Deuteronomy 22:28-29 — He must pay her father fifty shekels of silver. He must marry the young woman... He can never divorce her.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the firstborn of unclean animals (like a donkey)?
o: It must be redeemed with a lamb or have its neck broken
It must be given to the foreigner
Women of the Bible.e: Exodus 13:13 — Redeem with a lamb every firstborn donkey, but if you do not redeem it, break its neck.

Women of the Bible.q: How many years was a field to be planted before it rested?
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 25:3 — For six years sow your fields... but in the seventh year the land is to have a year of sabbath rest.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law concerning the treatment of the elderly?
o: They should be supported by the state
Stand up in the presence of the aged and show respect for the elderly
They should live in the temple
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 19:32 — Stand up in the presence of the aged, show respect for the elderly and revere your God.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the release of debts?
Women of the Bible.e: Deuteronomy 15:1 — At the end of every seven years you must cancel debts.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the 'tithe' of the land?
o: A tenth of the produce belongs to the Lord
A fifth of the produce belongs to the Lord
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 27:30 — A tithe of everything from the land... belongs to the Lord; it is holy to the Lord.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the lighting of the lamps in the Tabernacle?
o: They were to be lit only on the Sabbath
They were to be kept burning from evening till morning before the Lord continually
They were lit only during the day
They were never to be extinguished
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 24:2-3 — To keep the lamps burning continually... from evening till morning.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding a newborn animal for sacrifice?
o: It must remain with its mother for seven days and from the eighth day it will be acceptable
It must be one year old
It must be two years old
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 22:27 — For seven days it is to remain with its mother. From the eighth day on, it will be acceptable.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the 'Feast of Tabernacles' and where the people lived?
o: They lived in temporary shelters (booths) for seven days
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 23:42 — Live in temporary shelters for seven days.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the 'Sabbath for the Land'?
o: The land was to rest every 50 years
The land was to rest every 7 years
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 25:4 — But in the seventh year the land is to have a year of sabbath rest.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the 'Year of Jubilee' and property?
o: Property was to return to its original owner (the family's ancestral land)
Property was given to the king
Property was given to the priest
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 25:13 — In this Year of Jubilee everyone is to return to their own property.

Women of the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the 'Tithe of the Herd and Flock'?
o: Every fifth animal belongs to the Lord
Every tenth animal that passes under the shepherd's rod is holy to the Lord
Only the firstborn belonged to the Lord
The owner chose the best animal
Women of the Bible.e: Leviticus 27:32 — Every tenth animal that passes under the shepherd’s rod—will be holy to the Lord.

Animals in the Bible.q: Who was the king of Israel when Samaria was captured by the Assyrians?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 17:6 — In the ninth year of Hoshea, the king of Assyria captured Samaria.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Hezekiah and reigned for 55 years?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 21:1 — Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem fifty-five years.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Manasseh and was assassinated by his own officials?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 21:19-23 — Amon’s officials conspired against him and assassinated the king in his palace.

Animals in the Bible.q: Who was the king of Assyria who threatened Hezekiah and Jerusalem?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 18:13 — Sennacherib king of Assyria attacked all the fortified cities of Judah.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Manasseh and did evil like his father, but did not repent?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 21:18-20 — Amon son of Manasseh became king... He did evil in the eyes of the Lord, as his father Manasseh had done.

Animals in the Bible.q: Who was the king of Judah who was the son of Joash and was assassinated at Lachish?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 14:19 — They conspired against him (Amaziah) in Jerusalem and he fled to Lachish, but they sent men to Lachish after him and killed him there.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Hezekiah and was twelve years old when he became king?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 21:1 — Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king.

Animals in the Bible.q: Who was the king of Judah who was the son of Manasseh and reigned for 2 years?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 21:19 — Amon was twenty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem two years.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which king of Israel was the first to rule the northern kingdom?
Animals in the Bible.e: 1 Kings 12:20 — They sent and called him to the assembly and made him (Jeroboam) king over all Israel.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which Psalm says, 'And I said, Oh that I had wings like a dove! for then would I fly away, and be at rest'?
Animals in the Bible.e: Psalm 55:6 — Oh that I had wings like a dove!

Animals in the Bible.q: Who was the wife of King Hezekiah?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 21:1 — His (Manasseh's) mother’s name was Hephzibah.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which woman was the mother of King Manasseh?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 21:1 — His mother’s name was Hephzibah.

Animals in the Bible.q: Who was the mother of King Manasseh?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 21:1 — His mother’s name was Hephzibah.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did Noah send out first from the ark?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 8:7 — He sent out a raven, and it kept flying back and forth.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal spoke to Balaam?
Animals in the Bible.e: Numbers 22:28 — Then the Lord opened the donkey’s mouth, and it said to Balaam...

Animals in the Bible.q: What kind of animal swallowed Jonah?
Animals in the Bible.e: Jonah 1:17 — Now the Lord provided a huge fish to swallow Jonah.

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the first animal mentioned in the Bible?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 3:1 — Now the serpent was more crafty than any of the wild animals. (Actually Gen 1:21 mentions great sea creatures first, but Serpent is a common answer)

Animals in the Bible.q: Samson used the jawbone of which animal to kill a thousand Philistines?
Animals in the Bible.e: Judges 15:15 — Finding a fresh jawbone of a donkey, he grabbed it and struck down a thousand men.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did David kill while protecting his father's sheep?
Animals in the Bible.e: 1 Samuel 17:34-36 — Your servant has killed both the lion and the bear.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did Jesus ride into Jerusalem?
Animals in the Bible.e: Matthew 21:7 — They brought the donkey and the colt and placed their cloaks on them for Jesus to sit on.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal is the devil compared to in 1 Peter 5:8?
Animals in the Bible.e: 1 Peter 5:8 — Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.

Animals in the Bible.q: What bird did the Holy Spirit descend as during Jesus' baptism?
Animals in the Bible.e: Luke 3:22 — And the Holy Spirit descended on him in bodily form like a dove.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal is described in Job 41 as having scales like rows of shields?
Animals in the Bible.e: Job 41:1-15 — Can you pull in Leviathan with a fishhook?... Its back has rows of shields tightly sealed together.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal's hair did John the Baptist wear?
Animals in the Bible.e: Matthew 3:4 — John’s clothes were made of camel’s hair.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animals did the prodigal son feed when he was in need?
Animals in the Bible.e: Luke 15:15 — So he went and hired himself out... who sent him to his fields to feed pigs.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did Peter find a coin in the mouth of?
Animals in the Bible.e: Matthew 17:27 — Go to the lake and throw out your line. Take the first fish you catch; open its mouth and you will find a four-drachma coin.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal is used to represent the tribe of Judah?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:9 — You are a lion’s cub, Judah.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal is used to represent the tribe of Benjamin?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:27 — Benjamin is a ravenous wolf.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal is Issachar compared to in Jacob's blessing?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:14 — Issachar is a rawboned donkey lying down among the sheepfolds.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal is Dan compared to in Jacob's blessing?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:17 — Dan will be a snake by the roadside, a viper along the path.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animals came out of the Nile as the second plague?
Animals in the Bible.e: Exodus 8:3 — The Nile will teem with frogs.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Israelites sacrifice for the Passover?
Animals in the Bible.e: Exodus 12:5 — The animals you choose must be year-old males without defect, and you may take them from the sheep or the goats.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal in Proverbs 30 has no king, yet they all go forth in ranks?
Animals in the Bible.e: Proverbs 30:27 — Locusts have no king, yet they advance together in ranks.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did Samson find a swarm of bees and honey in the carcass of?
Animals in the Bible.e: Judges 14:8 — He turned aside to look at the lion’s carcass, and in it he saw a swarm of bees and some honey.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animals did the Egyptians pursue the Israelites with?
Animals in the Bible.e: Exodus 14:9 — The Egyptians—all Pharaoh’s horses and chariots, horsemen and troops—pursued the Israelites.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal is associated with the goddess of the Ephesians in Acts 19 (indirectly via a shrine)?
Animals in the Bible.e: Artemis (Diana) was often associated with the deer/stag. (Harder question)

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Gibeonites use to deceive Joshua, along with worn-out sacks and wineskins?
Animals in the Bible.e: Joshua 9:4 — They loaded their donkeys with worn-out sacks and old wineskins.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal did Jesus say it is easier for to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter heaven?
Animals in the Bible.e: Matthew 19:24 — It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animals were the Israelites not allowed to eat according to Leviticus 11?
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 11:7 — And the pig... for you it is unclean.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which bird is mentioned in Psalm 103 as a symbol of renewed youth?
Animals in the Bible.e: Psalm 103:5 — So that your youth is renewed like the eagle’s.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal is described as 'mocking at fear' and 'not frightened' in Job 39?
Animals in the Bible.e: Job 39:19-22 — Do you give the horse its strength?... It laughs at fear, afraid of nothing.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Israelites find in the desert that bit them, leading to the bronze serpent?
Animals in the Bible.e: Numbers 21:6 — Then the Lord sent fiery serpents among the people.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the King of Assyria send to Samaria to kill the people who did not fear the Lord?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 17:25 — The Lord sent lions among them and they killed some of the people.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal did Daniel's friends not have to face, but Daniel did?
Animals in the Bible.e: Daniel 6:16 — So the king gave the order, and they brought Daniel and threw him into the lions’ den.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal's blood was used to dip Joseph's coat in?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 37:31 — Then they got Joseph’s robe, slaughtered a goat and dipped the robe in the blood.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal is used in the Bible to describe someone who is stubborn?
Animals in the Bible.e: Psalm 32:9 — Do not be like the horse or the mule, which have no understanding.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Israelites make to carry their sins on the Day of Atonement?
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 16:10 — But the goat chosen by lot as the scapegoat shall be presented alive before the Lord.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animals did the sorcerers of Egypt turn their staffs into?
Animals in the Bible.e: Exodus 7:12 — Each one threw down his staff and it became a snake.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the prophet Isaiah say would lie down with the lamb in the future kingdom?
Animals in the Bible.e: Isaiah 11:6 — The wolf will live with the lamb.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal did the prophet Isaiah say would eat straw like the ox?
Animals in the Bible.e: Isaiah 11:7 — The lion will eat straw like the ox.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the false prophets in the time of Micaiah make horns of?
Animals in the Bible.e: 1 Kings 22:11 — Now Zedekiah son of Kenaanah had made iron horns. (Representing an ox)

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Lord use to symbolize the kingdom of Greece in Daniel 8?
Animals in the Bible.e: Daniel 8:5 — A goat with a prominent horn between its eyes came from the west.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the Assyrian army in Isaiah 7?
Animals in the Bible.e: Isaiah 7:18 — In that day the Lord will whistle for flies from the Nile... and for bees from the land of Assyria.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal is mentioned as being 'not afraid of any' in Proverbs 30?
Animals in the Bible.e: Proverbs 30:30 — A lion, mighty among beasts, who retreats before nothing.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal is mentioned as being 'stately in his stride' in Proverbs 30 (KJV: greyhound)?
Animals in the Bible.e: Proverbs 30:29-31 — There are three things that are stately in their stride... a lion... a strutting rooster... a he-goat.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Issachar?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:14 — Issachar is a rawboned donkey.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Gad?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:19 — Gad will be attacked by a band of raiders, but he will attack them at their heels. (Often associated with a lion/warrior)

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Joseph?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:22 — Joseph is a fruitful vine. (KJV/Other versions sometimes compare him to a wild ox or bull in Deut 33:17)

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Dan?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:17 — Dan will be a snake by the roadside.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Benjamin?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:27 — Benjamin is a ravenous wolf.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal is mentioned as being 'without a king' in Proverbs 30?
Animals in the Bible.e: Proverbs 30:27 — Locusts have no king.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Judah?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:9 — You are a lion’s cub, Judah.

Animals in the Bible.q: What animal did the Lord use to describe the tribe of Asher?
Animals in the Bible.e: Genesis 49:20 — Asher’s food will be rich. (No animal specifically, but associated with richness)

Animals in the Bible.q: Where was the 'Pool of Bethesda' located?
Animals in the Bible.e: John 5:2 — Now there is in Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate a pool... called Bethesda.

Animals in the Bible.q: Where did Jesus find Peter and Andrew fishing?
Animals in the Bible.e: Matthew 4:18 — As Jesus was walking by the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers.

Animals in the Bible.q: Where did Jesus stay during the week of his passion?
Animals in the Bible.e: Matthew 21:17 — And he left them and went out of the city to Bethany, where he spent the night.

Animals in the Bible.q: How many days was Jonah in the belly of the great fish?
Animals in the Bible.e: Jonah 1:17 — Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.

Animals in the Bible.q: How many years did Manasseh reign in Jerusalem?
Animals in the Bible.e: 2 Kings 21:1 — Manasseh... reigned in Jerusalem fifty-five years.

Animals in the Bible.q: How many sheep did the shepherd leave to find the one lost sheep?
Animals in the Bible.e: Luke 15:4 — Doesn’t he leave the ninety-nine in the open country?

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal's mother's milk was it forbidden to cook the young animal in?
Animals in the Bible.e: Exodus 23:19 — Do not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the 'lex talionis' or the law of retaliation found in the Torah?
Animals in the Bible.e: Exodus 21:24 — Eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.

Animals in the Bible.q: According to the Law, what should an Israelite do if they found their enemy's ox or donkey wandering off?
Animals in the Bible.e: Exodus 23:4 — If you come across your enemy’s ox or donkey wandering off, be sure to return it.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which bird was considered unclean and could not be eaten?
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 11:13 — These are the birds you are to regard as unclean... the eagle, the vulture...

Animals in the Bible.q: Which of these was a forbidden mixture according to the Law?
o: Yoking an ox and a donkey together
Sowing a field with two kinds of seed
Animals in the Bible.e: Deuteronomy 22:9-10 — Do not plant two kinds of seed in your vineyard... Do not plow with an ox and a donkey yoked together.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which of these was NOT a requirement for a king of Israel according to the Law?
o: He must not acquire great numbers of horses
He must not be older than 40
He must not take many wives
Animals in the Bible.e: Deuteronomy 17:15-17 — The law mentions he must be an Israelite, not accumulate horses or wives, and not accumulate silver and gold. It says nothing about age.

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the law concerning the edges of a garment?
o: They must have tassels with a blue cord
Animals in the Bible.e: Numbers 15:38 — Make tassels on the corners of your garments, with a blue cord on each tassel.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which of these was a forbidden food?
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 11:22 — You may eat any kind of locust, katydid, cricket or grasshopper. Bees (and most other winged insects) were unclean.

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the firstborn of clean animals?
o: They must be given to the poor
They must be sacrificed to the Lord
They must be kept for breeding
Animals in the Bible.e: Numbers 18:17 — But you must not redeem the firstborn of a cow, a sheep or a goat; they are holy.

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the punishment for a person who blasphemed the Name of the Lord?
o: Working in the temple for life
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 24:16 — Anyone who blasphemes the name of the Lord is to be put to death. The entire assembly must stone them.

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the 'Guilt Offering' (Trespass Offering)?
o: It required restitution plus a fifth of the value
It was only for the king
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 6:5 — He must make full restitution, add a fifth of the value to it and give it all to the owner.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which animal was NOT allowed as a burnt offering?
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 1:2-14 — Burnt offerings could be from the herd, the flock, or birds (doves/pigeons). Pigs were unclean.

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the 'Fat of the Fat Tail' of a sheep?
o: It was eaten by the worshiper
It was burned on the altar as an offering to the Lord
It was given to the poor
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 3:9 — He is to bring... the entire fat tail cut off close to the backbone. (It was burned)

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the slaughter of a cow or sheep and its young?
o: Do not slaughter a cow or a sheep and its young on the same day
The young must be slaughtered first
The mother must be slaughtered first
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 22:28 — Do not slaughter a cow or a sheep and its young on the same day.

Animals in the Bible.q: Which of these was a 'Sacred Assembly' day?
o: The first and seventh day of Unleavened Bread
The first and eighth day of Tabernacles
Animals in the Bible.e: Leviticus 23 describes these as days of sacred assembly and rest.

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the 'Second Passover' in Numbers 9?
o: Those who were unclean or on a journey could celebrate it one month later
It was only for the priests
It was for those who missed the first one for any reason
Animals in the Bible.e: Numbers 9:10-11 — They are to celebrate it on the fourteenth day of the second month at twilight.

Animals in the Bible.q: What was the law regarding the 'Fringes' (Tassels) in Numbers 15?
o: They were to remind the people to obey all the commands of the Lord
They were only for the High Priest
Animals in the Bible.e: Numbers 15:39 — You will have these tassels to look at and so you will remember all the commands of the Lord.

Bible Geography.q: Who was the king of Persia who allowed the Jews to return to Jerusalem?
Bible Geography.e: Ezra 1:1-2 — Cyrus king of Persia issued a proclamation throughout all his dominion.

Bible Geography.q: Who was the king of Judah when the Babylonians destroyed Jerusalem?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 25:1-7 — Zedekiah was the last king of Judah before the exile.

Bible Geography.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Josiah and was carried off to Egypt?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 23:31-34 — Pharaoh Necho put Jehoahaz in chains... and took him to Egypt.

Bible Geography.q: Who was the king of Judah who was the son of Jehoram and reigned for only one year?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 8:25-26 — Ahaziah son of Jehoram king of Judah began to reign... He reigned in Jerusalem one year.

Bible Geography.q: Which king of Babylon saw the handwriting on the wall?
Bible Geography.e: Daniel 5:1 — King Belshazzar gave a great banquet for a thousand of his nobles.

Bible Geography.q: Who was the king of Egypt who attacked Jerusalem during the reign of Rehoboam?
Bible Geography.e: 1 Kings 14:25 — In the fifth year of King Rehoboam, Shishak king of Egypt attacked Jerusalem.

Bible Geography.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Josiah and was also called Mattaniah?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 24:17 — He (the king of Babylon) made Mattaniah, Jehoiachin’s uncle, king in his place and changed his name to Zedekiah.

Bible Geography.q: Who was the king of Israel that reigned during the time of the prophet Elijah and the contest on Mount Carmel?
Bible Geography.e: 1 Kings 18:16-17 — So Obadiah went to meet Ahab and told him, and Ahab went to meet Elijah.

Bible Geography.q: Who was the king of Babylon who released Jehoiachin from prison?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 25:27 — Evil-Merodach king of Babylon... released Jehoiachin king of Judah from prison.

Bible Geography.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Jehoiakim and was carried off to Babylon?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 24:12 — Jehoiachin king of Judah... surrendered to him (the king of Babylon).

Bible Geography.q: Who was the king of Judah who was the son of Jehoram and reigned for 1 year?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 8:25-26 — Ahaziah son of Jehoram king of Judah began to reign... and he reigned in Jerusalem one year.

Bible Geography.q: Which king of Judah had his eyes put out by the king of Babylon?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 25:7 — They killed the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes. Then they put out his eyes.

Bible Geography.q: Which Psalm says, 'By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down, yea, we wept, when we remembered Zion'?
Bible Geography.e: Psalm 137:1 — By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down.

Bible Geography.q: Who was the wife of King Solomon who was the daughter of Pharaoh?
Bible Geography.e: 1 Kings 3:1 — Solomon made an alliance with Pharaoh king of Egypt and married his daughter.

Bible Geography.q: Which woman was the mother of King Amaziah of Judah?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 14:2 — His mother’s name was Jehoaddan; she was from Jerusalem.

Bible Geography.q: Who was the wife of King Amaziah of Judah?
Bible Geography.e: 2 Kings 15:2 — His (Uzziah's) mother’s name was Jecoliah; she was from Jerusalem.

Bible Geography.q: What animal did the widow of Zarephath have a small amount of oil and what else?
Bible Geography.e: 1 Kings 17:12 — I don’t have any bread—only a handful of flour in a jar and a little olive oil in a jug. (Not an animal question per se, but in that context)

Bible Geography.q: In what city was Jesus born?
Bible Geography.e: Matthew 2:1 — Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea.

Bible Geography.q: Where did Moses receive the Ten Commandments?
Bible Geography.e: Exodus 19:20 — The Lord descended to the top of Mount Sinai and called Moses.

Bible Geography.q: In what river was Jesus baptized?
Bible Geography.e: Matthew 3:13 — Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John.

Bible Geography.q: What was the name of the garden where Jesus prayed before his crucifixion?
Bible Geography.e: Matthew 26:36 — Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane.

Bible Geography.q: On what mountain did the ark of Noah come to rest?
Bible Geography.e: Genesis 8:4 — The ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.

Bible Geography.q: What sea did the Israelites cross to escape from Egypt?

Numbers & Dates.q: Which king of Judah was only eight years old when he began to reign?
