Numbers & Dates.e: Acts 13:21 — God gave them Saul... who ruled forty years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years was David in Jerusalem?
Numbers & Dates.e: 2 Samuel 5:5 — In Jerusalem he reigned over all Israel and Judah thirty-three years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many sons of David were born in Hebron?
Numbers & Dates.e: 2 Samuel 3:2-5 — Six sons were born to David in Hebron.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many men of Israel did David count in his census?
Numbers & Dates.e: 2 Samuel 24:9 — In Israel there were eight hundred thousand able-bodied men.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many men of Judah did David count in his census?
Numbers & Dates.e: 2 Samuel 24:9 — And in Judah five hundred thousand.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years did Asa reign?
Numbers & Dates.e: 1 Kings 15:10 — He reigned in Jerusalem forty-one years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years did Jehoshaphat reign?
Numbers & Dates.e: 1 Kings 22:42 — He reigned in Jerusalem twenty-five years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years did Baasha reign?
Numbers & Dates.e: 1 Kings 15:33 — He reigned in Tirzah twenty-four years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years did Omri reign?
Numbers & Dates.e: 1 Kings 16:23 — He reigned twelve years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years did Ahab reign?
Numbers & Dates.e: 1 Kings 16:29 — He reigned over Israel in Samaria twenty-two years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years did Jehu reign?
Numbers & Dates.e: 2 Kings 10:36 — The time that Jehu reigned over Israel in Samaria was twenty-eight years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years did Jeroboam II reign?
Numbers & Dates.e: 2 Kings 14:23 — He reigned forty-one years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years did Uzziah (Azariah) reign?
Numbers & Dates.e: 2 Kings 15:2 — He reigned in Jerusalem fifty-two years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many years did Hezekiah reign?
Numbers & Dates.e: 2 Kings 18:2 — He reigned in Jerusalem twenty-nine years.

Numbers & Dates.q: How many witnesses were required to convict someone of a capital crime?
Numbers & Dates.e: Deuteronomy 17:6 — On the testimony of two or three witnesses a person shall be put to death.

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law regarding the offering of a firstfruit of the dough?
o: It was given to the poor
It was given to the priest
It was eaten by the family
Numbers & Dates.e: Numbers 15:20 — Present a loaf from the first of your ground meal as an offering. (Given to the priests)

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law regarding the inheritance of daughters if there were no sons?
o: They could inherit but had to marry within their father's tribe
The property went to the uncle
Numbers & Dates.e: Numbers 36:6 — They may marry anyone they please as long as they marry within their father’s tribal clan.

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law regarding a Nazirite and their hair?
o: They must cut it every month
No razor may be used on their head during the period of their vow
Numbers & Dates.e: Numbers 6:5 — During the entire period of their Nazirite vow, no razor may be used on their head.

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law regarding a Nazirite and grapes?
o: They must not eat or drink anything that comes from the grapevine
They must give grapes to the priest
There was no law about grapes
Numbers & Dates.e: Numbers 6:3-4 — They must not drink wine... They must not eat fresh grapes or raisins... nothing that comes from the grapevine.

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law regarding the 'Redemption Price' in the Year of Jubilee?
o: It was calculated based on the number of years until the next Jubilee
It was based on the person's age
It was decided by the priest
Numbers & Dates.e: Leviticus 25:25-27 — The price was based on the years of harvest remaining until the Jubilee.

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law concerning the 'Trial of Jealousy' in Numbers 5?
o: A woman suspected of adultery drank 'bitter water'
The man had to pay a fine
A priest decided by a lot
Numbers & Dates.e: Numbers 5:11-31 describes the ritual involving the water of bitterness.

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law regarding the 'Priestly Blessing' in Numbers 6?
o: It was 'The Lord bless you and keep you'
It was 'The Lord is my shepherd'
Numbers & Dates.e: Numbers 6:24-26 — The Lord bless you and keep you; the Lord make his face shine on you...

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law regarding the 'Silver Trumpets' in Numbers 10?
o: Two silver trumpets were used for calling the community and for having the camps set out
They were used by the King only
They were used by the choir only
Numbers & Dates.e: Numbers 10:2 — Make two trumpets of hammered silver.

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law regarding the 'Red Heifer' in Numbers 19?
o: Its blood was used for anointing
Its ashes were used to make the 'water of cleansing' for those who touched a dead body
It was a burnt offering for the King
It was a sin offering for the High Priest
Numbers & Dates.e: Numbers 19:2-9 — The ashes are to be kept... for use in the water of cleansing; it is for purification from sin.

Numbers & Dates.q: What was the law regarding the 'Cities of Refuge' and the High Priest?
o: The person could leave only after the death of the High Priest
The person was free after 7 years
The person was free after 1 year
Numbers & Dates.e: Numbers 35:25 — The person must stay there until the death of the high priest, who was anointed with the holy oil.

Old Testament Law.q: Who was the king of Moab who sacrificed his eldest son on the city wall to turn the tide of battle?
Old Testament Law.e: 2 Kings 3:26-27 — Then he (the king of Moab) took his firstborn son... and offered him as a sacrifice on the city wall.

Old Testament Law.q: Which Psalm says, 'Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me'?
Old Testament Law.e: Psalm 51:10 — Create in me a clean heart, O God.

Old Testament Law.q: Which Psalm says, 'The statutes of the Lord are right, rejoicing the heart: the commandment of the Lord is pure, enlightening the eyes'?
Old Testament Law.e: Psalm 19:8 — The statutes of the Lord are right.

Old Testament Law.q: Which Psalm says, 'Wash me throughly from mine iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin'?
Old Testament Law.e: Psalm 51:2 — Wash me throughly from mine iniquity.

Old Testament Law.q: Which Psalm says, 'Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean: wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow'?
Old Testament Law.e: Psalm 51:7 — Purge me with hyssop.

Old Testament Law.q: What animal was provided as a substitute for Isaac when Abraham was about to sacrifice him?
Old Testament Law.e: Genesis 22:13 — Abraham looked up and there in a thicket he saw a ram caught by its horns.

Old Testament Law.q: What animal did the people of Lystra think Paul and Barnabas were (Zeus and Hermes), leading them to bring what to sacrifice?
Old Testament Law.e: Acts 14:13 — The priest of Zeus... brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates.

Old Testament Law.q: What animal's skin was used for the covering of the Tabernacle?
Old Testament Law.e: Exodus 26:14 — Make for the tent a covering of ram skins dyed red, and over that a covering of the hides of sea cows. (KJV: badger skins)

Old Testament Law.q: Which city was known as the 'City of Palms'?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 34:3 — The Valley of Jericho, the City of Palms.

Old Testament Law.q: Where did the Lord tell Abraham to go and sacrifice Isaac?
Old Testament Law.e: Genesis 22:2 — Go to the region of Moriah. Sacrifice him there.

Old Testament Law.q: Which book of the Bible contains the most detail about the laws for sacrifices and priests?
Old Testament Law.e: The Book of Leviticus is primarily concerned with laws for priests and sacrifices.

Old Testament Law.q: What is the first of the Ten Commandments?
o: You shall have no other gods before me
Old Testament Law.e: Exodus 20:3 — You shall have no other gods before me.

Old Testament Law.q: According to the Law, how often was the Year of Jubilee to occur?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 25:10 — Consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim liberty throughout the land.

Old Testament Law.q: Which commandment is the first one with a promise?
Old Testament Law.e: Ephesians 6:2 (quoting Exodus 20) — 'Honor your father and mother'—which is the first commandment with a promise.

Old Testament Law.q: What were the Israelites forbidden to wear, according to Deuteronomy 22:11?
o: Clothes made of wool and linen woven together
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 22:11 — Do not wear clothes of wool and linen woven together.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the corners of a field during harvest?
o: They must be left unharvested for the poor and the foreigner
They must be burned as a sacrifice
They must be given to the priest
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 19:9 — Do not reap to the very edges of your field.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law concerning a man who had newly married?
o: He must go to war immediately
He was exempt from military service for one year
He must stay in the temple for 40 days
He must pay a special tax
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 24:5 — If a man has recently married, he must not be sent to war... For one year he is to be free to stay at home and bring happiness to the wife he has married.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding interest on loans to a fellow Israelite?
Old Testament Law.e: Exodus 22:25 — If you lend money to one of my people among you who is needy, do not treat it like a business deal; charge no interest.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding a garment taken as a pledge for a loan?
o: It must be returned by sunset
It becomes the property of the lender after 3 days
It must be burned if not redeemed
It must be given to the priest
Old Testament Law.e: Exodus 22:26 — If you take your neighbor’s cloak as a pledge, return it by sunset.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the 'Shema', the central prayer of the Law?
o: Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one
Our Father which art in heaven
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 6:4 — Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law concerning someone who found a lost item?
o: They must return it to the owner
They must give it to the priest
They must wait 30 days before keeping it
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 22:1-3 — Do not ignore it but be sure to take it back to its owner.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the punishment for a son who was persistently stubborn and rebellious and would not listen to his parents?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 21:18-21 — Then all the men of his town are to stone him to death.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the king of Israel supposed to do when he took the throne?
o: Write for himself a copy of the Law on a scroll
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 17:18 — He is to write for himself on a scroll a copy of this law.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding a bird's nest found with a mother and her young?
o: You may take the young but must let the mother go
You must take the mother but leave the young
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 22:6-7 — You may take the young, but be sure to let the mother go.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the gleanings of a vineyard?
o: They should be gathered for the owner
They should be left for the poor and the foreigner
They should be used for wine for the priest
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 19:10 — Do not go over your vineyard a second time or pick up the grapes that have fallen. Leave them for the poor and the foreigner.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding weights and measures?
o: They should be based on the Egyptian system
They must be honest and accurate
They must be checked by the priest every month
A 5% margin of error was allowed
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 19:35-36 — Use honest scales and honest weights.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law concerning a man who died without a son?
o: His property went to the king
His brother was to marry the widow to carry on his name
The property went to the priest
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 25:5 — Her husband’s brother shall take her and marry her and fulfill the duty of a brother-in-law to her.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding a prisoner of war whom an Israelite man wanted to marry?
o: She had to shave her head, trim her nails, and mourn for a month
She had to become a slave first
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 21:11-13 — She shall shave her head, trim her nails and put aside the clothes she was wearing when captured... after that you may go to her and be her husband.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law concerning the move of a boundary stone?
o: It was encouraged to expand land
It could only be done by a priest
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 27:17 — Cursed is anyone who moves their neighbor’s boundary stone.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the payment of a hired worker?
o: Pay them at the end of the week
Pay them before sunset on the day they worked
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 24:15 — Pay them their wages each day before sunset, because they are poor and are counting on it.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law concerning a person with leprosy?
o: They were to live in the temple
They must wear torn clothes, let their hair be unkempt, cover their lower face and cry out, 'Unclean! Unclean!'
They must be put to death
They were treated like everyone else
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 13:45 — The person with such an infectious disease must wear torn clothes, let their hair be unkempt, cover the lower part of their face and cry out, 'Unclean! Unclean!'

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the eating of blood?
o: It was only for the priests
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 17:10 — I will set my face against any Israelite... who eats blood and I will cut them off.

Old Testament Law.q: Which of these was a 'clean' sea creature?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 11:9 — Of all the creatures living in the water... you may eat any that has fins and scales.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding a house with mildew (leprosy) that could not be cleansed?
o: It must be torn down and its stones, timber and plaster carried to an unclean place
It must be given to the poor
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 14:45 — It must be torn down—its stones, timbers and all the plaster—and taken out of the town to an unclean place.

Old Testament Law.q: Which of these was a required sacrifice for a person who sinned unintentionally?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 4:2 — When anyone sins unintentionally and does what is forbidden...

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the 'Wave Offering'?
o: It was waved before the Lord by the priest
It was thrown into the sea
It was burned on the altar
It was given to the poor
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 7:30 — He is to bring the fat... and wave it before the Lord as a wave offering.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the Showbread (Bread of the Presence)?
o: Twelve loaves were placed on the table every Sabbath
It was eaten by the congregation
It was burned as a sacrifice
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 24:5-8 — Set them in two rows, six in a row... This bread is to be set out before the Lord regularly, Sabbath after Sabbath.

Old Testament Law.q: Who was allowed to eat the Showbread after it was replaced?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 24:9 — It belongs to Aaron and his sons, who are to eat it in a holy place.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the ransom of a person's life?
o: Everyone 20 years or older was to give a half-shekel as an offering to the Lord
Only the rich had to pay
It was based on the person's weight in gold
Old Testament Law.e: Exodus 30:12-15 — Each one must pay the Lord a ransom for his life... a half shekel.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the anointing oil of the Tabernacle?
o: It could be used by anyone
It was holy and not to be poured on anyone's body or made for common use
It was sold to raise money
Old Testament Law.e: Exodus 30:31-32 — It is sacred, and you are to consider it sacred. Do not pour it on anyone’s body and do not make any concoction with the same formula.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the skin of the burnt offering?
o: It belonged to the priest who offered it
It was given to the poor
It was used for the Tabernacle
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 7:8 — The priest who offers a burnt offering... may keep the hide for himself.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the grain offering (Minchah)?
o: It must not contain leaven or honey, but must contain salt
It was only for the Day of Atonement
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 2:11-13 — Do not include any yeast or honey... Season all your grain offerings with salt.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding a peace offering brought as a 'Thank Offering'?
o: It could be eaten over three days
It must be eaten on the day it is offered; none should be left till morning
It was only offered at night
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 7:15 — The meat of their fellowship offering of thanksgiving must be eaten on the day it is offered.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the 'Vow Offering' or 'Freewill Offering'?
o: It must be eaten on the same day
It could be eaten on the day offered and the following day, but none left for the third day
It was strictly for the priest
It was only for the Levites
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 7:16-17 — It may be eaten on the day he offers it as well as the next day... any meat left over to the third day must be burned.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the 'Breast' of the peace offering?
o: It was a 'Wave Offering' given to Aaron and his sons
It was given to the poor
It was eaten by the worshiper
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 7:31-34 — The breast belongs to Aaron and his sons.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the 'Right Thigh' of the peace offering?
o: It was a 'Heave Offering' given to the priest who offered the blood and fat
It was given to the poor
It was eaten by the worshiper
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 7:32-33 — You are to give the right thigh of your fellowship offerings to the priest as a contribution.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the High Priest and his hair?
o: He must not let his hair become unkempt
He must wear it in a bun
There was no law about it
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 21:10 — The high priest... must not let his hair become unkempt or tear his clothes.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the priest and physical defects?
o: No man of the descendants of Aaron who had a defect could approach to offer the food of his God
Only the High Priest had this law
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 21:17-21 — No man of your descendants... who has a physical defect may approach to offer the food of his God.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the eating of 'Holy Food' by a stranger?
o: No one outside a priest's family may eat it
Only the King could eat it
It was only for the High Priest
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 22:10 — No one outside a priest’s family may eat the sacred offering.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding an animal with a defect intended for a sacrifice?
o: It was acceptable if the defect was small
You must not offer the Lord anything with a defect; it will not be accepted on your behalf
It was preferred for sin offerings
Only the High Priest could decide
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 22:20 — Do not bring anything with a defect, because it will not be accepted on your behalf.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding work on the Day of Atonement (Yom Kippur)?
o: You must not do any work at all; it is a sabbath of rest
Work was allowed for the priests only
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 23:28 — Do not do any work on that day, for it is the Day of Atonement.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding an Israelite who sold himself to a fellow Israelite?
o: He was to be treated as a slave
He was not to be treated as a slave, but as a hired worker or temporary resident
He was a slave for life
He could never be set free
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 25:39-40 — Do not make them work as slaves. They are to be treated as hired workers or temporary residents.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding an Israelite who sold himself to a foreigner?
o: He retained the right of redemption; a relative could buy him back
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 25:47-49 — They retain the right of redemption... One of their relatives may redeem them.

Old Testament Law.q: Which of these was a curse promised for disobedience in Leviticus 26?
o: Your soil will not yield its crops
I will send wild animals among you
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 26:16-22 describes these and other punishments.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding a person who made a special vow to the Lord?
o: A set value was placed on the person based on age and sex for redemption
They could never change their mind
It was only for the rich
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 27:2-8 — The law gives specific values in silver shekels for different ages and genders.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the replacement of a holy animal with another?
o: If someone tries to replace it, both the original and the replacement become holy
It was allowed for a small fee
Only the priest could do it
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 27:33 — If he does make a substitution, both the animal and its substitute become holy and cannot be redeemed.

Old Testament Law.q: Which book of the Law contains the 'Second Law' or the repetition of the Law?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy means 'Second Law' in Greek.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the 'False Witness' in Deuteronomy 19?
o: Do to them as they intended to do to their brother
They were forgiven if they confessed
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 19:19 — Then do to the false witness as that witness intended to do to the other party.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the 'Roof of a House' in Deuteronomy 22?
o: It must have a parapet (railing) so no one would fall from it
It must be made of stone
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 22:8 — When you build a new house, make a parapet around your roof.

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding the 'Divorce' in Deuteronomy 24?
o: A man could write a certificate of divorce and give it to his wife
Only the woman could initiate it
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 24:1 — He writes her a certificate of divorce, gives it to her and sends her from his house.

Old Testament Law.q: Which book contains the dietary laws for Israel?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 11

Old Testament Law.q: What animal was used as a scapegoat?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 16:10

Old Testament Law.q: How many years was the land to rest in the Sabbatical year?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 25:4

Old Testament Law.q: What was the Year of Jubilee?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 25:10

Old Testament Law.q: Who were the only two spies who brought a good report?
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 14:6-9

Old Testament Law.q: What did Moses lift up in the wilderness to heal the people from snakes?
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 21:9

Old Testament Law.q: Which book's name means 'Second Law'?
Old Testament Law.e: Etymology of Deuteronomy

Old Testament Law.q: What is the 'Shema'?
o: Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 6:4

Old Testament Law.q: Where were the Ten Commandments stored?
o: Inside the Ark of the Covenant
Old Testament Law.e: Exodus 25:16

Old Testament Law.q: What was the penalty for gathering wood on the Sabbath?
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 15:32-36

Old Testament Law.q: How many cities of refuge were there?
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 35:6

Old Testament Law.q: Who were the sons of Aaron who offered strange fire?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 10:1

Old Testament Law.q: What was the primary duty of the Levites?
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 3:5-10

Old Testament Law.q: Which tribe did not receive a land inheritance?
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 18:20

Old Testament Law.q: What did a person with leprosy have to cry out?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 13:45

Old Testament Law.q: What was the purpose of the Cities of Refuge?
o: For those who killed someone accidentally
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 35:11

Old Testament Law.q: What bird was used in the cleansing of a leper?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 14:4

Old Testament Law.q: How many times was the blood sprinkled on the Day of Atonement?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 16:14

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law regarding a neighbor's landmark?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 19:14

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law of the Nazirite?
o: No wine, no hair cutting, no dead bodies
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 6:1-8

Old Testament Law.q: What was the punishment for a rebellious son?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 21:18-21

Old Testament Law.q: What should be done if a man finds his neighbor's ox wandering?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 22:1

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about wearing mixed fabrics?
o: Do not wear wool and linen mixed
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 22:11

Old Testament Law.q: What was the tassels on the corners of garments for?
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 15:38-39

Old Testament Law.q: How many witnesses were required for a death penalty?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 17:6

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about a king's horses?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 17:16

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about a king's wives?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 17:17

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about a king's wealth?
o: He must not accumulate large amounts of gold/silver
He must give it all away
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 17:17

Old Testament Law.q: What was the priest's portion of a sacrifice?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 7:31-34

Old Testament Law.q: What happened to the firstborn of clean animals?
Old Testament Law.e: Numbers 18:17

Old Testament Law.q: What was the 'Tithe'?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 27:30

Old Testament Law.q: How often was the tithe of the third year given?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 14:28

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law of the 'Levirate Marriage'?
o: A man marries his deceased brother's widow
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 25:5

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Gleaning'?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 19:9-10

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'False Witness'?
o: Do to him as he intended for his brother
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 19:16-19

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Weight and Measures'?
Old Testament Law.e: Leviticus 19:35-36

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Interest'?
o: Do not charge interest to a fellow Israelite
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 23:19

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Vows'?
o: When you make a vow, do not delay in paying it
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 23:21

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Pledges'?
o: Do not take a millstone as a pledge
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 24:6

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Kidnapping'?
o: The kidnapper shall be put to death
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 24:7

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Wages'?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 24:15

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Fathers and Children's Sins'?
o: Each shall die for his own sin
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 24:16

Old Testament Law.q: What was the maximum number of lashes for a crime?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 25:3

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Muzzling an Ox'?
o: Do not muzzle an ox while it treads grain
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 25:4

Old Testament Law.q: What was the law about 'Writing the Law on Doorposts'?
Old Testament Law.e: Deuteronomy 6:9

Worship & Music.q: Who was the king who built the first Temple in Jerusalem?
Worship & Music.e: 1 Kings 6:1 — Solomon began to build the house of the Lord.

Worship & Music.q: Which king of Judah was known for repairing the Temple after finding the Book of the Law?
Worship & Music.e: 2 Kings 22:8 — Hilkiah the high priest said to Shaphan the secretary, 'I have found the Book of the Law in the temple of the Lord.'

Worship & Music.q: Which king of Judah was struck with leprosy for trying to burn incense in the Temple?
Worship & Music.e: 2 Chronicles 26:19-21 — Uzziah had a censer in his hand to burn incense... leprosy broke out on his forehead.

Worship & Music.q: Who was the king of Tyre who sent cedar trees and craftsmen to help Solomon build the Temple?
Worship & Music.e: 1 Kings 5:1 — Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon.

Worship & Music.q: Which king of Judah was the son of Uzziah and built the Upper Gate of the Temple?
Worship & Music.e: 2 Kings 15:32-35 — Jotham son of Uzziah became king... He rebuilt the Upper Gate of the temple of the Lord.

Worship & Music.q: Which king of Persia issued a decree that the Temple in Jerusalem be rebuilt, as recorded in Ezra?
Worship & Music.e: Ezra 1:1 — In the first year of Cyrus king of Persia... the Lord moved the heart of Cyrus.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 100:4 — Enter into his gates with thanksgiving.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'Oh that men would praise the Lord for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 107:8 — Oh that men would praise the Lord for his goodness.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'Sing unto the Lord a new song; for he hath done marvellous things'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 98:1 — Sing unto the Lord a new song.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'Open thou my lips; and my mouth shall shew forth thy praise'?
Which Psalm says, 'I will bless the Lord at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 34:1 — I will bless the Lord at all times.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'Yet the Lord will command his lovingkindness in the daytime, and in the night his song shall be with me'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 42:8 — Yet the Lord will command his lovingkindness.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, in the mountain of his holiness'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 48:1 — Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me: and to him that ordereth his conversation aright will I shew the salvation of God'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 50:23 — Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'In God I will praise his word, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what flesh can do unto me'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 56:4 (and 11) — In God I will praise his word.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed: I will sing and give praise'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 57:7 — My heart is fixed, O God.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'But I will sing of thy power; yea, I will sing aloud of thy mercy in the morning'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 59:16 — But I will sing of thy power.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'Because thy lovingkindness is better than life, my lips shall praise thee'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 63:3 — Because thy lovingkindness is better than life.

Worship & Music.q: Which Psalm says, 'My soul shall be satisfied as with marrow and fatness; and my mouth shall praise thee with joyful lips'?
Worship & Music.e: Psalm 63:5 — My soul shall be satisfied.

Worship & Music.q: Which woman killed Sisera by driving a tent peg through his temple?
Worship & Music.e: Judges 4:21 — Jael took a tent peg and a hammer and drove the peg through his temple.

Worship & Music.q: Which woman was a prophetess who recognized the infant Jesus in the Temple?
Worship & Music.e: Luke 2:36 — There was also a prophet, Anna, the daughter of Penuel.

Worship & Music.q: What animal did Aaron fashion out of gold for the Israelites to worship?
Worship & Music.e: Exodus 32:4 — He took what they handed him and made it into an idol cast in the shape of a calf.

Worship & Music.q: What animal is Naphtali compared to in Jacob's blessing?
Worship & Music.e: Genesis 49:21 — Naphtali is a doe set free that bears beautiful fawns.

Worship & Music.q: What animal did the Shulammite's beloved resemble in Song of Solomon?
Worship & Music.e: Song of Solomon 2:9 — My beloved is like a gazelle or a young stag.

Worship & Music.q: What animal did the prophet Isaiah say the nursing child would play near?
Worship & Music.e: Isaiah 11:8 — The infant will play near the cobra’s den.

Worship & Music.q: What river did Joshua and the Israelites cross to enter the Promised Land?
Worship & Music.e: Joshua 3:17 — The whole nation had completed the crossing on dry ground.

Worship & Music.q: In what city did the walls fall after the Israelites blew trumpets?
In what city was the Great Temple of Artemis located?
Worship & Music.e: Acts 19:27 — The temple of the great goddess Artemis will be discredited.

Worship & Music.q: In what city did Paul and Silas sing in prison at midnight?
Worship & Music.e: Acts 16:25 — About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God.

Worship & Music.q: In what city did Paul and Barnabas separate?
Worship & Music.e: Acts 15:36-40 — They had such a sharp disagreement that they parted company... Paul chose Silas and left.

Worship & Music.q: Where did the Lord tell the prophet Malachi to prophecy?
Worship & Music.e: Malachi addressed the people in Jerusalem after the rebuilding of the temple.

Worship & Music.q: Which offering was to be completely burned on the altar?
Worship & Music.e: Leviticus 1:9 — The priest is to burn all of it on the altar. It is a burnt offering.

Worship & Music.q: Which animal's fat was the priest supposed to burn on the altar?
o: The fat that covers the inner parts
The long lobe of the liver
Worship & Music.e: Leviticus 3:3-4 — All the fat that is on the internal organs... both kidneys with the fat on them... and the long lobe of the liver.

Worship & Music.q: What was the law regarding the fire on the altar?
o: It should be lit only for sacrifices
It must be kept burning; it must not go out
It was lit by the sun
It was only for the High Priest to light
Worship & Music.e: Leviticus 6:13 — The fire must be kept burning on the altar continuously; it must not go out.

Worship & Music.q: What was the law regarding the 'Peace Offering' (Shelamim)?
o: It was entirely for the priest
Part was for the Lord (fat), part for the priest (breast/thigh), and part for the worshiper to eat
It was only for the High Priest
It was a fast, not a feast
Worship & Music.e: Leviticus 7:11-34 — The peace offering involved a shared meal between God, the priest, and the worshiper.

Worship & Music.q: Which of these was NOT a blessing promised for obedience in Leviticus 26?
o: Five of you will chase a hundred
Worship & Music.e: Leviticus 26:4-8 mentions rain, peace, and victory in battle, but doesn't mention chariots as a blessing.
